Technical Implementation and Features: Dapper provides an array of APIs for tasks such as service invocation, state management, and more, which simplify coding complexities and allow developers to concentrate on core logic. These tools aid in tasks such as managing Dapper upgrades, ensuring security, and maintaining system stability, which are crucial for smooth operations in regulated or large-scale environments.

Future Developments and Innovations: The session concludes with a forward-looking perspective on the expanding capabilities of Dapper, geared towards serverless workflow execution and AI-driven workflows. title: API Collaboration - Building APIs Together: a Multi-Player Strategy
sortTitle: api collaboration - building apis together: a multi-player strategy
description: APIs are rarely built alone—and they shouldn't be. In this session, we’ll explore how a team-based, “multiplayer” approach to API development can eliminate versioning issues, reduce redundant work, and dramatically speed up your delivery cycle. You’ll learn how Postman’s unified platform empowers teams to collaborate seamlessly across every stage of the API lifecycle—from design to deployment.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Collaborative API Development: The speaker, a professional at Postman, emphasizes the shift from code-first to API-first in the technology sector, highlighting the transition towards viewing API development as foundational rather than an afterthought. This shift underlines the importance of building APIs prior to other software components, which boosts developers' productivity by clarifying API functionalities early in the development cycle.

Challenges in API Development: Developing APIs involves various complex and intertwined tasks such as prototyping, coding, testing, securing, and documenting, which traditionally are spread across different tools and teams. This fragmentation often leads to inefficiencies, miscommunication, and delays. A collaborative approach is suggested to mitigate these issues, promoting efficient development processes and reducing potential security vulnerabilities.

The Role of Postman in Streamlining API Workflows: While many organizations utilize tools like Postman to facilitate their workflow, usage is often limited with developers working in isolated environments. The speaker criticizes this 'single player mode' of working and advocates for a more integrated use of Postman where collaboration and sharing are at the forefront, enhancing the development process and minimizing duplications and errors.

Advantages of a Collaborative Approach: Emphasizing on collaboration, the speaker points out that both producing and consuming APIs typically requires interactions with a broad spectrum of stakeholders including front and back end engineers, QA engineers, and product managers. Effective collaboration across these groups through tools that facilitate easy access to APIs in development can greatly accelerate timelines and improve end results.

Using Postman's API Development Platform: Postman's platform offers a comprehensive suite of tools that support the entire API lifecycle—design, building, testing, documentation—which consolidates different aspects of API development into a singular, unified workflow. This approach not only enhances productivity but also helps in maintaining high standards of security and compliance essential for modern software development.

Conclusion: Concluding, the speaker advocates for a switch to more collaborative methods in API development, leveraging platforms like Postman that facilitate a highly integrated and cooperative environment. This method is presented as a conduit to achieving faster development cycles, higher API quality, and stronger security postures within teams and organizations.

aiDescription:

Security and Accessibility: Security and user accessibility were highlighted as foundational elements of MCP's design. Feedback and Development: The session concluded with a call for feedback from developers, indicating that MCP is still in a phase of active development and refinement. aiDescription:
Introduction to Vibe Coding: The focus of the discussion is on "Vibe Coding," a concept gaining traction in the developer community, particularly among those not deeply versed in technical coding aspects. This method has been brought to the limelight via a viral social media post, showcasing its innovative approach where ideas take precedence over complicated coding procedures.

Core Philosophy of Vibe Coding: The essence of Vibe Coding is about simplifying and visualizing the creative process in programming. It is particularly useful for individuals who want to quickly iterate on visual elements and concepts without worrying about the intricacies of code, enabling even those who aren't tech-savvy to bring their ideas to life through technology.

Accessibility and Ease of Use: Vibe Coding democratizes coding by making technology readily accessible to a broader audience, including those outside the professional development sphere. It allows users to articulate a problem or idea and explore iterations before potentially handing off more complex development tasks to programmers. This feature fosters collaboration and inclusivity across various skill levels within the tech industry.

User Insights and Experiences: Participants shared their personal experiences with Vibe Coding, highlighting how it allows them to swiftly execute and visualize projects with minimal coding knowledge. Real-life applications include creating functional prototypes like websites that can be commercially viable, thereby proving its effectiveness beyond just a conceptual framework.

Future Potential and Applications: Looking ahead, Vibe Coding is anticipated to further break barriers by integrating with other advanced tech solutions, enhancing its functionality and scope of application. Users envision utilizing Vibe Coding in conjunction with other technologies to create more complex and refined outputs, expanding its usefulness across various sectors and project types.

Conclusion and Call to Action: The discussion concludes by encouraging more individuals to explore Vibe Coding, pointing out its potential to transform mere ideas into tangible products without extensive coding knowledge. It invites tech enthusiasts and professionals alike to experience firsthand the benefits and potentialities of this innovative approach, suggesting its rising influence in shaping future tech development methodologies.

aiDescription:
Partnership Initiative: Coldplay partnered with Microsoft and Pixel Labs in Seattle to develop a unique film for their latest album, Moon Music. This project involves a unique collaboration where visual artists were invited to interpret sections of the album, enriching the musical experience with diverse visual representations.

Creative Process: The initiative tapped into the talents of 150 visual artists who were given segments of the album to visually interpret. This approach has been likened to a "reverse soundtrack," where the visual elements are crafted in response to the music.

Interactive Fan Engagement: To broaden fan participation, Coldplay envisioned a platform where fans could interactively engage by remixing video content. To facilitate this, Coldplay utilized AI technologies provided by Microsoft, allowing fans without professional editing skills to contribute creatively.

AI Utilization: The project heavily integrated AI to assist in the remixing process, enabling a dynamic interaction between the audio elements of the album and user-generated visual content. This AI integration highlights a pioneering use of technology in music and visual arts.

Technical Execution: The technical aspect involved analyzing extensive video content using Azure Video Analyzer which was instrumental in managing and adapting the vast amounts of visual data effectively. Moreover, environmental and economic impacts were considered, ensuring the project was conducted responsibly.

Conclusion: This endeavour not only fosters a new level of fan interaction but also sets a precedent for the use of AI in merging visual art with music, reflecting Coldplay's innovative approach to music production and audience engagement.

aiDescription:

title: Under the hood and into the magic of GitHub Copilot
sortTitle: under the hood and into the magic of github copilot
description: GitHub Copilot, the original Copilot, is built on top of foundational large language models and makes use of nearly every pattern to help you code faster and in the flow. But, have you ever wanted to know what happens as GitHub Copilot turns prompts into code suggestions and what happens afterwards? Hear from the GitHub Copilot team on how they built this revolutionary tool and adapt to the evolving landscape where new models and patterns for development are emerging at an unprecedented pace.
aiDescription:
Introduction to GitHub Copilot: The session started with an overview of GitHub Copilot functionalities, demonstrating how it effectively integrates into development workflows. GitHub Copilot utilizes AI to automate coding tasks, enhance code understanding, and streamline coding processes. The tool was introduced as a significant asset for developers, aiming to simplify and accelerate the coding experience.

Exploring Agent Mode: The discussion moved towards the Agent Mode provided by GitHub Copilot. This mode enables the software to undertake coding tasks autonomously, simulating a virtual coding partner. By setting tasks, the agent operates independently, generating code, and executing tasks in a draft pull request format. This mode allows the developers to review the agent's work, mirroring a real-life code review process between team members.

Integration with MCP Servers: The session highlighted the capabilities of integrating GitHub Copilot with MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers. This integration allows the agent to interact with external services, expanding its ability to pull in context and execute tasks that involve external APIs and services. This feature is instrumental in broadening the areas where GitHub Copilot can be effectively utilized, increasing its applicability in more complex scenarios.

Context Management and Knowledge Graph: An essential part of enhancing GitHub Copilot's efficiency is its context management capability. Through MCP servers and custom instructions files, developers can feed specific contexts to the tool, enabling it to make more informed decisions and suggestions. Additionally, the ongoing development of a knowledge graph aims to refine context understanding further, allowing for more accurate and relevant code generation.

Future Developments and Enhancements: As GitHub Copilot continues to evolve, future enhancements are focused on improving its understanding of complex contexts and integrating widely with various coding repositories and environments. These developments aim to make GitHub Copilot a more robust and versatile tool for a wide array of programming tasks and environments, maintaining its ease of use and integration.

speakerNames: Shabala Natarajan, Martin Simecek, Richa Thakur
title: Improving App Health with Health Modeling and Chaos Engineering
sortTitle: improving app health with health modeling and chaos engineering
description: Reliability engineering is the process of taking a structured approach to improving Application Health. This involves identifying critical flows in the application and building a health model that helps you understand if everything is working, and where potential problems arise. Join us in exploring real-life customer scenarios with Reliability Engineering, Chaos Studio experiments and the new Azure Monitor Health Models service.
aiDescription:
**Introduction to the Session:** The session opened with a warm welcome, discussing the critical nature of application health and its impact on business operations. The importance of maintaining resilient systems in a world where applications drive business functions was emphasized.

**Focus on App Health and Resilience:** The discussion centered on the necessity of App Health management, illustrating the various dimensions from platform, service, architectural to code levels. A specific focus was on understanding why app resilience is crucial, not just as an afterthought but as a mandatory aspect of development and deployment processes.

**Concept of Health Modeling and Chaos Engineering:** Health modeling and chaos engineering were identified as key strategies in enhancing app health. These approaches involve simulating potential disruptions and planning proactive measures, ensuring stability and robust operational continuity.

**Microsoft's Reliability Engineering Process:** Microsoft’s approach to reliability was highlighted, showing how they integrate health modeling and chaos engineering into their system frameworks. The session discussed how resilience is built into the Azure platform, allowing customers to deploy workloads securely and reliably.

**Practical Demonstrations and Tools:** The session included live demonstrations of health modeling and chaos engineering in action, utilizing Microsoft's technologies. Tools like Azure Monitor and Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) were discussed, showing how they contribute to overseeing and improving app health.

**Interactive Case Studies and Customer Successes:** Examples from real-life scenarios where Microsoft has guided customers through resilience planning were discussed. These cases illustrated the practical applications of the theories discussed, showing the tangible benefits of prioritizing app health in various industries.

In conclusion, the session reinforced the importance of proactive health management in applications, emphasizing the integration of resilience at every level of development and deployment. Microsoft’s tools and strategies for building, maintaining, and testing robust applications were outlined, providing a comprehensive understanding of the path to enhanced app health.

description:

Join us on a journey from catalog to cloud as we explore the capabilities and limitations of reasoning models using an enterprise catering application with complex scheduling and inventory constraints. title: Accelerate AI on NVIDIA RTX AI PCs with Windows ML
sortTitle: accelerate ai on nvidia rtx ai pcs with windows ml
description: Learn to build and deploy next-gen applications featuring digital humans, agents, and media generation—all with maximum AI performance. NVIDIA RTX AI PCs and workstations offer an end-to-end, GPU-accelerated platform for private, on-device AI. See practical demos and get step-by-step guidance on integrating powerful AI features at peak performance into your Windows apps.
aiDescription:
Introduction: The speaker from the GeForce product team begins by addressing the audience on the topic of accelerating PC applications using RTX GPUs and experimental uses of generative AI on these platforms.

Generative AI Advancements: The discussion highlights how Generative AI is transforming PC software by introducing new experiences such as writing assistance, creative tools, and digital agents. Despite the innovative progress, the deployment of AI-enabled features presents unique challenges for developers, especially regarding the high computational requirements and varied PC ecosystem.

Developer Tools and Challenges: Developers face difficulties in quickly starting AI experimentation due to the fragmented PC ecosystem, which includes multiple silicon types and software libraries. These challenges lead to a maintenance burden over time. However, tools like Windows ML facilitate application deployment by providing a unified API that works across different execution providers.

NVIDIA's RTX GPU Capabilities: RTX GPUs are highlighted for their superior AI performance, featuring dedicated Tensor cores designed specifically for AI computations. The discussion also covers how NVIDIA's hardware paired with software like CUDA and Tensor RT, optimizes AI model execution on these GPUs.

Windows ML and Tensor RT: Windows ML is introduced as a solution that simplifies the deployment of applications on Windows, automatically adjusting to the hardware present and downloading necessary files. It supports multiple model types and ensures optimal performance through updates directly from NVIDIA.

Conclusion: The summary wraps up by emphasizing the comprehensive suite offered by NVIDIA for AI development and deployment across different platforms, which includes the use of SDKs and tools that integrate AI into applications seamlessly. The speaker invites the audience to explore further post-session and provides resources for continued learning and development on NVIDIA's platform.
location: Arch, 705 Pike, Level 4, Hub, Theater B

description: Unlock exciting new possibilities for your Copilot Agent in Power Apps with Copilot Studio and new SDK's. Learn how to use Copilot Studio and the Power Apps Component framework to customize agent chat in model-driven apps. Enhance in-app chat with new topics, knowledge sources, connectors, and custom prompts. Extend and personalize your app using new Copilot and PCF JavaScript APIs. aiDescription:
Introduction to Session: The session commenced with an overview of enhancing and customizing agents within Power Apps using Copilot Studio and USDKS. The focus was on leveraging artificial intelligence (AI) to improve user experience and streamline app functionalities.

AI Adoption Focus: A significant emphasis was placed on AI adoption and integration within Power Apps. The discussion highlighted the customization and usage of agents to optimize business processes, enhance productivity, and provide immediate assistance to users through automated features like form filling and data visualization.

Interactive Agent Features: The session delved into the capabilities of the interactive agent deployed within the Copilot chat. The agent is designed to respond contextually within different app sections, offering tailored assistance based on the specific area of the app the user is interacting with.

Agent Customization and Connectivity: An in-depth explanation was provided on customizing the interactive agent using Copilot Studio, which could involve adding specific knowledge or prompts that can guide the user more effectively. Additionally, the use of APIs to connect custom business logic and external data sources was discussed to show how agents can pull in dynamic content or trigger actions.

Practical Application and Demonstration: Demonstrations included scenarios where agents were used to fetch data, fill forms autonomously, provide interactive summaries, and even suggest actions based on AI-driven data analysis. This part of the session showcased how users could leverage agent capabilities to automate tasks and enhance data interaction within apps.

Conclusion and Resources: The session concluded with directions on how users could explore these capabilities further through specific Power Apps documentation and guides. Attendees were encouraged to engage with the system to understand the practical implications of AI integration in customizing agents and improving app functionalities.

title: Making Agent Memory Better and Agent Actions Faster with TypeAgent
sortTitle: making agent memory better and agent actions faster with typeagent
description:
We are trying to create human-like memory with super-human precision and recall for agent conversations.  We will demo our memory implementation outperforming current systems at answering questions about past conversations such as "what step were we on in building the photo montage?" 

We are trying to apply agent interfaces to all applications. To make this possible, we have created a system that can distill language models into logical systems that can handle most user requests.
aiDescription:
Introduction and Project Overview: The session opens with a warm welcome, and the speaker, Steve Luco, expresses enthusiasm about presenting advancements in agent memory and actions. The primary focus is on the 'type agent' project which aims to integrate logical systems like operating systems and databases with stochastic models such as large language models.

Principles of Agent Design: During the development, three guiding principles emerged. The first is distilling models into logical structures that minimize errors and enhance performance. The second principle revolves around using structure to control information density, which improves the focus and efficiency of models. The third, although not demonstrated due to time constraints, involves using logical structures to enable collaborative problem-solving among various computational models and human inputs.

Demonstration Insights: A series of demos illustrate how these principles are applied in agent technologies. The demos showcase agents performing tasks with enhanced memory capabilities and responsive actions, like recalling details from past conversations and integrating these into ongoing interactions, thus exemplifying real-time data handling and decision-making efficiency.

Agent Integration and Interaction: The discussion covers potential integrations of this technology into existing platforms, stressing that the current demonstrations are part of a technological show and not yet ready for product inclusion. These integrations aim to make agents capable of remembering intricate details over long periods and interacting more naturally with users.

Concluding Thoughts: Steve concludes by encouraging the audience to explore the AI Foundry Labs page for more detailed information and access to resources supporting type agent technology. He reiterates the effectiveness and potential of the developed structures in enhancing agent interactions and operational efficiency.

Thank You and Closure: The session ends with a note of gratitude and an invitation to further engage with the project's developments through the provided links and resources.

title: Boost Your App’s Success with the Latest Microsoft Store Features
sortTitle: boost your app’s success with the latest microsoft store features
description: Explore the new tools for maximizing your reach on Windows. We’ll cover the latest improvements to app submission—including improved account onboarding and faster certification —along with new discovery methods like web acquisition, targeted app promotions, and semantic search. You’ll also see demos of the improved health and acquisition reports and A/B testing capabilities. We will close with best practices to engage and retain your users.
aiDescription:
Introduction to New Features: The session, led by a Principal Software Engineer from the Microsoft Store Client team, delved into the latest and upcoming features for developers in the Microsoft Store. The primary discussion focused on the application lifecycle, which includes onboarding, submission, certification, and analytics enhancements. The talk also highlighted new features designed to increase an app's reach and engagement among end-users.

Store Updates and Mission: The mission of the Microsoft Store is to provide safe and trustworthy content to all Windows users, ensuring every app is scanned for malware and optimized for performance. The store's aim is not only to facilitate seamless app discovery across various platforms but also to improve the client experience on Windows, thereby increasing overall customer engagement and app discoverability.

Developer Onboarding and Support: The session announced zero-cost onboarding for individual developers to reduce barriers and enhance accessibility. For companies, although the fee remains unchanged, a temporary workaround and advanced support through the Microsoft Store Fast Track Program were presented, which offers extensive support from Microsoft engineers during the app publishing process.

Enhancements in Publishing and Insights: Improvements include actionable certification reports with detailed guidance and visual aids to help developers overcome issues during app certification. Additionally, revamped analytics capabilities in Partner Center now provide deeper insights into app performance, helping developers prioritize and address critical issues more effectively.

Privacy and User Experience Upgrades: The discussion also touched on user experience enhancements such as streamlined privacy policy hosting, where developers can now input their privacy policy directly in the Partner Center, and Microsoft will manage the hosting. Other user-oriented updates include more intuitive product page layouts, which help to boost conversion rates and enhance overall user satisfaction.

Closing and Engagement Encouragement: The talk concluded with an invitation for developers to leverage these new tools and features to enhance their apps' reach, engagement, and retention. Developers were encouraged to provide feedback and engage with the Microsoft team through various channels to help shape the future developments of the Microsoft Store.

Introduction to Document Processing: The discussion began with an overview of document processing tools and their relevance in handling the vast volumes of unstructured data within enterprises. The speakers highlighted the significant challenges businesses face with data management and the crucial role document processing plays in various industry sectors such as finance, healthcare, and government.

New Document Processing Tools: The conversation introduced new AI-powered tools designed to streamline document processing workflows. These include the new out-of-the-box document processor agent, which simplifies the setup of document workflows, and custom tools for creating more tailored processing functionalities. The session promised demonstrations of these tools, emphasizing ease of use and customization.

Demonstration of Document Processor Agent: A detailed demonstration showcased the setup and functionality of the new document processor agent. The agent captures, classifies, extracts, and integrates document content autonomously while allowing for human oversight and validation. The demonstration detailed each step, from installation and configuration to execution, illustrating the agent's capability to handle complex workflows efficiently.

Customization and Configuration: The speakers explored the agent's customization options, which are vital for adapting the tool to specific enterprise needs. They discussed the configuration wizard that guides users through setting up and tailoring the agent according to particular business requirements. Participants were shown how to train the agent using document samples, adjust extraction settings, and validate extracted data.

Integration with Other Services: The integration capabilities of the document processing tools with other enterprise systems and applications like SharePoint and Outlook were discussed. This integration facilitates seamless data flow and accessibility, enhancing overall process efficiency within business ecosystems.

Interactive Capabilities and Future Enhancements: The session concluded with insights into the interactive and conversational capabilities of the new tools, which mimic interactions with human agents, providing status updates and responses to queries regarding document processing tasks. The speakers hinted at future enhancements that would continue to improve document processing efficiency and customization, demonstrating the evolving nature of AI tools in enterprise environments.

Capabilities of AMD: AMD's offerings are highlighted, including a suite of CPUs, GPUs, networking hardware, and cluster-level design. Secure and Trusted Solutions: AMD places emphasis on security and trust by supporting features like trusted launch on Azure. Developer Support and Ecosystem: The session underscores AMD’s commitment to supporting developers through open-source solutions, robust developer ecosystems, and frequent updates. title: A practical guide to building agentic workflows
sortTitle: a practical guide to building agentic workflows
description: AI agents are revolutionizing how we build software, but creating effective agentic workflows requires rethinking our approach to APIs and process design. In this lightning talk, we'll unpack how MCPs and APIs form the backbone of successful agentic systems. Expect practical strategies for decomposing complex workflows into discrete, agent-friendly steps and explore the critical differences between human-focused and machine-focused API design.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Agentic Workflows: The session began with an introduction to agentic workflows, focusing on how they empower agents to act autonomously and interface seamlessly with various tools. Unlike traditional linear workflows, which are deterministic and brittle, agentic workflows are modular, reactive, and capable of abstract reasoning.

Practical Guide to Implementation: The discussion transitioned into a practical guide on implementing these workflows. It emphasized the importance of breaking away from linear thinking by understanding the goals, available tools, and potential actions that an AI agent might take. This approach allows for externalizing logic into systems that agents can use on demand.

Enhancing Capabilities through Abstraction: Further exploration of abstraction highlighted its role as a superpower in agentic workflows, enabling agents to make decisions dynamically. This is achieved through a layer that coordinates tools in real-time, transforming AI into a transformative decision-making layer.

Example of Practical Application: An example was provided using an autonomous customer service agent within Zendesk. This agent handles tickets autonomously if information is sufficient, or escalates them when necessary. The process involves a loop where agents learn from interactions to improve future responses, illustrating a dynamic interplay between automation and human oversight.

Adaptability through MCP Protocol: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) was introduced as an essential component for creating adaptable, modular, and intelligent systems. MCP acts like a universal adapter in the AI world, allowing agents to communicate with different systems and tools without needing custom configurations, thus preventing vendor lock-in and promoting scalability.

Conclusion and Takeaways: The session concluded with key takeaways on how agentic workflows leverage AI and toolsets to create dynamic, flexible systems that improve over time through continuous interaction and learning. This not only enhances automation but enables a substantial shift towards more adaptive and intelligent systems in customer support and beyond.
location: Arch, 705 Pike, Level 4, Hub, Theater B

aiDescription:

Product Creation from Natural Language: In addressing these issues, the speaker discusses the creation of functional products derived from simple natural language prompts, emphasizing the difficulty of such a task. The development process involves several challenges, especially in translating natural language and image inputs into functional components like HTML and JavaScript code.

Tool Integration and Automation: The session includes a detailed discussion on integrating and automating development tools. The speaker mentions building a multi-agent system that uses natural language processing to automate UI generation, which can be integrated with popular tools like GitHub Copilot. This system largely aids in enhancing development efficiency by streamlining component configuration and setup.

Demonstration of Technology: A live demonstration showcases the practical application of these concepts, where the speaker demonstrates how the system processes input through various agents within a server to generate usable UI code. This system supports both text and image inputs, which it can validate and translate into UI elements, significantly expediting the development process.

Implementation and Architecture: The architecture of the multi-agent system is explored, indicating how different agents handle specific tasks such as input validation, layout generation, and UI code generation. This structure ensures that the system is modular and each component can be independently managed and optimized.

Conclusion: The session concludes with reflections on how this automated approach to handling UI elements can save significant time for developers, allowing them to focus on more complex and innovative tasks. The speaker highlights the adaptability of the solution to various frameworks and components, making it a versatile tool for a wide range of development environments.

sortTitle: build the next gen of ai apps with .net: models, data, agents, & more
description: .NET is transforming how developers build AI native applications and agents. With deep integrations throughout the AI ecosystem, a streamlined development process with Microsoft.Extension.AI, templates to jumpstart development, and the ability to leverage and build the latest in Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Agents, .NET is the go to for AI development. Come see how all of this works together and how we are evolving .NET for developers to build interactive agents that communicate silently and at lightning speed. Tell the front-end agent to book travel to the Build conference, and your calendar invites and expense report confirmations start appearing. Agents are transforming business processes and this session explores how .NET and AI can optimize workflows and integrate agents into applications. Learn to use familiar tools to build and manage intelligent agents, enhancing productivity and communication within organizations.
aiDescription:
Introduction to WebVTT: The discussion starts with a focus on using AI to build next-generation apps, specifically in the context of a conference where attendees have gathered early in the morning after previous sessions.

Evolution of AI Technologies: The conversation shifts to recent advancements in AI, emphasizing the launch of GPT technologies that brought generative AI to general users. The rapid adoption rate and the exponential growth in usage within a short span underline the significant impact of these technologies.

AI's Accelerating Development: The discourse continues about the rate at which generative AI applications are being integrated into various sectors, mentioning how certain foundational AI tools have shed their 'experimental' status to enter general availability.

Practical Applications and Demonstrations: The presentation includes practical demonstrations showing how developers can leverage AI in building complex applications. It discusses integrating AI functionalities like classification, summarization, and sentiment analysis using .NET.

Feedback and Interactive Elements: The event encourages feedback from the attendees, aiming to refine and enhance the AI tools based on user experience. It also includes interactive elements where audience members are actively engaging with the ongoing presentation through direct questions and feedback.

Closure and Future Events: The discussion ends with reminders for future events and an invitation for attendees to provide feedback, emphasizing the community-driven development approach to AI technology enhancement.

speakerNames: Mads Bolaris, Zankar Desai, Nate Moore
title: Building agents in Copilot Studio using Model Context Protocol, open standards and more
sortTitle: building agents in copilot studio using model context protocol, open standards and more
description: Join us for a session on building agents in Copilot Studio using Azure AI Foundry services. Learn to build advanced Copilot Studio agents by integrating Azure AI Search, the Azure model catalog, and Model-Context Protocol (MCP) into your agents.
aiDescription:

MCP facilitates seamless integration and communication among various software tools and systems, enhancing functionality and user experience in AI-driven environments.

Capabilities of MCP: Detailed explanation was given on how MCP operates akin to a USB-C port for applications, allowing them to connect with external tools and data sources effortlessly. This analogy highlighted the protocol’s versatility and essential role in expanding the potential applications of AI by providing a structured and standardized communication pathway.

Integration and Adaptability: Discussion included the integration of MCP with Azure AI Foundry and Copilot Studio, showing how these platforms utilize MCP for improving agent interactions and functionalities. This section emphasized the adaptability of MCP, demonstrating its ability to support a wide range of applications and its critical role in the evolving landscape of AI and software development.

Interactive Demonstrations and Use Cases: Several live demonstrations and practical use cases were presented to illustrate the real-world applications and benefits of MCP. These examples showcased how MCP could be leveraged in various scenarios to enhance data handling, improve agent responses, and facilitate the development of more sophisticated AI solutions.

Future Prospects and Community Engagement: The session concluded with a forward-looking perspective on the future developments anticipated for MCP. Attendees were encouraged to participate in community forums and contribute to the ongoing evolution of MCP, highlighting the collaborative nature of this technological advancement.

Closing Remarks: The event wrapped up with a Q&A session, providing attendees with the opportunity to clarify doubts and gather more insights directly from the experts. The emphasis was on continued learning and engagement with the MCP community to foster innovation and adoption in various tech domains.

aiDescription:

**Enhancing Mobility and Innovation**: The discussion shifted to the advantages of ARM systems on chips in terms of power efficiency and design flexibility for manufacturers, which allows for creating cooler, quieter devices with innovative form factors.

**Deep Dive into Developer Tools and Optimization**: The latter part of the session was technical, guiding developers on optimizing applications for ARM using tools and libraries provided by Microsoft and other contributors. sortTitle: deep research with langchain and deepseek r1
description:

It will provide the user a final markdown summary with all sources used. We will use DeepSeek R1 as the LLM, and Langchain to implement reflection architecture.

Introduction of Records: The speaker discusses how records, introduced as value-based types in contrast to traditional reference types, offer an optimized way of handling data with equality checks based on content rather than references. He emphasizes the ease of use and the automatic functionality enhancements like correct implementations of common methods.

Demonstration of Collection Expressions: Further, the session covers the advantages of using collection expressions in C#. These expressions provide a unified, clean syntax that works effectively across different collection types, ensuring the most efficient implementation by the compiler, often surpassing manually written code in performance.

Concluding Remarks: The speaker concludes by encouraging the audience to use these advanced features and invites them to discuss further at a designated meeting point. title: An overview of Windows AI Foundry
sortTitle: an overview of windows ai foundry
description: Whether you're starting out with AI development or looking to enhance your skills, learn more about Windows AI Foundry and its capabilities. We will cover an end-to-end build, customize, test, and deploy AI across client and cloud environments. aiDescription:
Windows Copilot Runtime: Provides developers with flexibility on what models can run and where, leveraging the Onyx runtime.
AI Toolkit for VS Code: Allows conversion, optimization, and quantization of models for application use.
Windows ML: Helps developers run AI workloads across CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs, providing execution providers, drivers, and runtime out of the box.
Developer Experience: Developers can source models, convert them to Onyx, optimize, and integrate them into applications using Windows ML NuGet.
AI Dev Gallery: An application available on Microsoft Store and GitHub, showcasing demos and samples of Windows AI Foundry.
Device Policies: Allows developers to specify where models run (CPU, GPU, NPU) based on efficiency, performance, and power settings.
Model Initialization: Involves referencing infrastructure packages, downloading execution providers, registering them, setting selection policies, and compiling models for inference.
Feedback from Developers: Developers have praised Windows ML for cutting development timelines, adding efficiency, and simplifying integration challenges.
Windows AI APIs: Includes diverse APIs for text generation, text recognition, image super resolution, image segmentation, object erase, and image description.
Customization Options: Developers can fine-tune models using Lora Fine Tuning or knowledge retrieval powered by semantic search.

sortTitle: unpacking windows ai foundry
description: Join Divya Venkatarmu and Jatinder Mann in unpacking Windows AI Foundry and MCP on Windows.
aiDescription:
Welcome and Introductions: The session begins with a warm welcome to the guests, who are experts ready to discuss various innovative topics including Windows, AI, and Foundry.

Windows AI Foundry Overview: The conversation shifts to an in-depth explanation of Windows AI Foundry, described as an evolution of Windows Copilot Runtime. It's highlighted as a unified platform designed to support the AI developer lifecycle across various hardware, with optimizations for seamless model selection, building, and deployment.

AI Development Support: The discussion emphasizes how Windows AI Foundry aids developers in every step of AI development, from integrating pre-built models to customizing them according to specific needs. The platform enables both local AI development and efficient cloud-client transitions.

Comparison with Azure AI Foundry: When asked about the differences between Windows AI Foundry and Azure AI Foundry, it's clarified that while both serve similar purposes, Windows AI Foundry focuses on local AI development, providing tools and features that are more aligned with personal and immediate computational needs, simplifying the usage of open-source models and proprietary adaptations.

Developer Features and Accessibility: The platform is designed to be accommodating for all levels of developers, from novices seeking straightforward API integrations to advanced users crafting custom models. There’s a special emphasis on the inclusion of varied toolsets like text intelligence and natural language processing (NLP), aiding in more personalized and efficient AI deployments.

Broader Integrations and Future Enhancements: The dialogue concludes with discussions on potential future enhancements that could integrate more deeply with cloud services, providing a spectrum of tools ranging from pre-built solutions to complex model development environments, thus promoting a robust and versatile AI development ecosystem within Windows.

title: Create with Windows AI: Practices For Safe, Responsible Experiences
sortTitle: create with windows ai: practices for safe, responsible experiences
description: Creating safe and innovative experiences with AI means using tools and techniques to avoid causing potential harm to customers while delivering on the promised capability they are looking for. Our AI experts will share concrete examples and recommendations. You will come away from the session knowing how to build more safeguards into your products as well as help you create a differentiated experience that is responsible by design.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Responsible AI: The speaker introduces herself as a member of the Windows Responsible AI team and extends a welcome at the Build conference. She emphasizes the innovations in AI and the importance of developing AI responsibly. The focus is on equipping the audience with tools and knowledge to implement AI responsibly in their projects.

Principles of Responsible AI: Microsoft’s approach to AI is structured around six principles: fairness, reliability and safety, privacy and security, inclusiveness, transparency, and accountability. These principles guide the development of AI technologies to ensure they are ethical and effective across diverse groups of people.

New Technologies and Tools: The discussion shifts to new hardware and software tools enhancing AI capabilities. The speaker introduces the Copilot Plus PC, equipped with a neural processing unit (NPU) that optimizes on-device AI tasks, offering benefits like reduced latency and improved privacy. Additionally, the Windows App SDK is highlighted, which includes various APIs that support local AI processes like text generation and image segmentation.

Implementation of AI Features: A live demonstration shows how developers can integrate and utilize AI functionalities using the AI Dev Gallery and the Windows Copilot Runtime APIs. The speaker walks through practical examples, showcasing how to use the APIs to manage outputs responsibly in AI applications, effectively handling potentially harmful content.

Responsible AI in Practice: The importance of balancing technological capabilities with ethical considerations is underscored. The speaker illustrates how the Windows platform enables developers to set parameters that prevent AI from engaging with or generating inappropriate content. This functionality is crucial for ensuring AI systems operate within ethical boundaries set by developers.

Conclusion and Continued Innovation: The closing remarks reiterate the commitment to responsible AI development, stressing its significance as both a business imperative and a moral obligation. The audience is encouraged to provide feedback and participate in continued learning and adaptation in AI technologies, ensuring that these advancements are aligned with societal values and needs.

aiDescription:

A local dev server allows for email previewing, boosting productivity and ease of use.

Incorporation of GitHub Copilot: GitHub Copilot's introduction revolutionized coding by providing inline suggestions, drastically reducing the time taken for routine coding tasks. The speaker demonstrates how to efficiently refactor code with Copilot’s suggestions, exemplifying a significant boost in productivity without the hassle of manual code adjustments.

Advanced Assistance with GitHub Copilot Chat: Transitioning to GitHub Copilot Chat, the speaker explores its ability to understand contextual inquiries about specific files within a project. Here, the Copilot serves more complex needs, such as automatically generating new email notification templates based on user-defined criteria, demonstrating a profound level of contextual understanding and adaptability.

Enhancing AI Capabilities with Agent Mode: The introduction of Agent Mode in GitHub Copilot Chat further enhances the AI’s capabilities, allowing it to perform more complex tasks akin to those of a software engineer, including testing changes for errors and executing code modifications across multiple files based on the developers' commands.

Integration of Model Context Protocol (MCP): The discussion shifts towards the integration of Model Context Protocol (MCP), which facilitates advanced interactions between AI models and external tools. This protocol allows for more seamless and secure connections to various services, demonstrated by connecting to a remote database to perform live data queries directly from within VS Code.

Deployment Demonstration on Azure: Finally, the speaker demonstrates deploying a serverless database on Azure, highlighting how Neon's integration counts toward Azure consumption commitments. This section illuminates the practical steps and ease of setting up a project directly through Azure, showing the real-world application and integration of these advanced tools in a cloud environment.

title: Enable Agentic AI Apps with a Unified Data Estate in Microsoft Fabric
sortTitle: enable agentic ai apps with a unified data estate in microsoft fabric
description: Artificial Intelligence is only as powerful as the data it is built upon. Microsoft Fabric marks a transformative leap by seamlessly unifying operational and analytical data estates, unlocking the potential for powerful custom AI applications. Discover new agentic AI scenarios that can be enabled by Microsoft Fabric. Demonstrations will feature agents powered by Azure AI Foundry—seamlessly integrating with Fabric Databases, Real Time Intelligence, Graph QL, OneLake, and Power BI. Discover how these cutting-edge technologies come together to deliver unparalleled customer experiences and learn how Microsoft Fabric is shaping the future of AI innovation
aiDescription:
Introduction to Microsoft Fabric: Microsoft Fabric, initially called the Unified Analytic Platform for Data, was announced two years ago. It aims to unite data in an unprecedented way, integrating SQL Database and Cosmos DB.
Integration of SQL and Cosmos DB: SQL and Cosmos DB are now part of the same fabric, providing a unified data platform for AI. This integration offers a seamless experience for developers.
Fabric Databases: Fabric databases are designed to be simple, autonomous, and optimized for AI. They allow developers to focus on training models and improving prompts without the overhead of managing data.
Cosmos DB in Fabric: Cosmos DB in Fabric is now in preview. It offers the same scalability, availability, and reliability as Azure Cosmos DB but is optimized for AI applications.
AI Features in Cosmos DB: Cosmos DB in Fabric supports built-in vector indexing and search, full-text and hybrid search, and Disk Ann for large vector indexing. These features make it a default choice for AI workloads.
Integration with One Lake: Data in Cosmos DB is automatically replicated into One Lake in delta parquet format. This allows seamless integration with other tools in Fabric, such as notebooks, Power BI, and data agents.
Operational and Analytical Integration: Fabric provides a unified way to access data for both operational and analytical purposes. This includes creating API interfaces for databases through GraphQL and using One Lake data for analytics.
Real-time Intelligence and Event Streaming: Fabric supports real-time intelligence and event streaming, allowing asynchronous processing of data and integration with AI agents for recommendations.
Power BI Integration: Fabric's integration with Power BI allows for real-time insights and recommendations, enhancing the ability to process and analyze data efficiently.
Partner Ecosystem: The Fabric community includes a growing number of partners who are leveraging the platform to build integrated, fast, and efficient solutions for various industries.

title: Imagine Cup Finalist: Argus, Empowering people with low vision with AI
sortTitle: imagine cup finalist: argus, empowering people with low vision with ai
description: Join this session for a live demo of Argus, a wearable device empowering individuals with low vision. Powered by Azure AI Speech and Azure OpenAI, it aids in object identification, facial recognition, and navigation through voice commands and haptic feedback, enhancing confidence and independence. As a finalist in the Imagine Cup—the premier global AI startup competition for student founders—Argus is pushing the boundaries of assistive technology.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Argus: The session began with introductions of the Imagine Cup winners who discussed their innovative project, Argus. Argus is designed to aid people with vision impairments by using AI technology. The inventors were driven to develop this project by personal connections to individuals with vision impairments, including their own grandparents.

Demonstration of Argus: A live demonstration of Argus was conducted to showcase its functionality. Argus accurately identified objects such as a Red Bull can and a coffee cup in the presenter's hands, highlighting its practical application in everyday scenarios that the visually impaired face, such as identifying medications and navigating environments.

Technical Aspects of Argus: Argus incorporates a camera module integrated into eyeglasses and uses AI models. These components are coupled by a compute module that carries out AI processing. The device operates on a hybrid model that utilizes both edge AI and cloud AI to process data and perform tasks efficiently.

Connectivity and Power Efficiency: Argus employs a unique system called YR for connectivity, which is significantly more power-efficient than traditional Wi-Fi, allowing for a lighter device with extended battery life. This system also ensures secure transmission of data between the camera and compute module.

Ethical and Privacy Considerations: Addressing ethical concerns, the development team emphasized that Argus processes and retains most data locally to safeguard privacy. Facial recognition data, for instance, is not uploaded to the cloud but processed locally, enhancing security and user trust.

Conclusion and Q&A: The session concluded with a Q&A segment where the presenters answered technical inquiries about Argus and discussed future enhancements. The team highlighted Argus's seamless integration into daily life and its utility for people with visual impairments, promoting independence and ease of use.
location: Arch, 705 Pike, Level 4, Hub, Theater B

title: Imagine Cup Finalist: Signvrse, AI-powered Sign Language Translation
sortTitle: imagine cup finalist: signvrse, ai-powered sign language translation
description: Join this session for a live demo of Signvrse, an AI-powered platform breaking communication barriers for the Deaf community. Using Azure AI Speech, it translates spoken language into text and 3D avatar-driven sign language in real time, making communication more accessible anywhere. As a finalist in the Imagine Cup—the premier global A startup competition for student founders—Signvrse is redefining inclusive technology through innovative uses of AI.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Signverse Presentation: Daniel introduces himself and briefly sets the stage for a discussion about global communication access and equality. His introduction serves as a segue into the broader theme of communication barriers faced by the deaf community. Daniel's personal experiences as a deaf individual underscore the reality of these challenges.

Global Deaf Community Statistics: Highlighting the extensive number of deaf individuals worldwide, Daniel mentions there are 1.5 billion globally, with around 40 million in Africa alone. This statistic emphasizes the significant impact communication barriers have on a large segment of the population.

Challenges Faced by the Deaf: Daniel outlines three major obstacles: the high costs of interpreting services, the lack of technical skills among the deaf for leveraging technology, and the absence of inclusive spaces for social interactions. These points illustrate the systemic issues that prevent equal access to communication for the deaf.

Signverse Solutions: Moving from problems to solutions, the presentation shifts focus to "Signverse," a company Daniel is representing. The team at Signverse is actively addressing these communication barriers by developing technologies that assist the deaf community, thereby fostering more inclusive communication environments.

Technology and Innovation at Signverse: The discussion delves into the specifics of the technologies being developed by Signverse, like TAP 360, an application that translates text and speech into sign language. This technology uses advanced techniques like motion capture to provide accurate and real-time translations, making communication more accessible for the deaf.

Community and Future Plans: Daniel reflects on the broader impact of these innovations and Signverse’s commitment to community engagement and inclusivity. Future plans include expanding their services and continuing advancements in their technology to further break down communication barriers for the deaf worldwide.

Framework and Tools Utilization: The development harnessed Azure AI Foundry's capabilities, employing a reliable framework that supports safe and efficient voice solutions. This was augmented with CICD pipelines using GitHub Actions and scaled via Azure Container Apps, demonstrating a robust setup for large-scale application deployment.

Enhanced Public Engagement: Hera AI, accessible through the Hera.space web portal, has engaged over 10,000 users since its launch. This initiative exemplifies how interactive AI can personalize and enhance public experience, broadening access to complex scientific data and space exploration insights.

Data Management and Real-Time Interaction: The application brilliantly handles telemetry data through Azora Cosmos DB, ensuring that Hera AI can share real-time mission data, images, and videos. This functionality not only underpins seamless conversations powered by Asia API management but also exemplifies cutting-edge data management in practice.

Comprehensive Development and Outreach: A noteworthy aspect of Hera AI's development is the collaboration across various sectors, including space agencies, AI research teams, and technology providers, to bring space closer to the general public. The AI's capability to provide conversational experiences and its integration with real-time data sharing platforms highlights a significant advancement in how AI can be used for educational and public engagement purposes.

Future Potential and Scalability: Looking forward, Hera AI represents a scalable model for similar applications in other fields. title: Get faster AI agent responses and low app latency with Azure Managed Redis
sortTitle: get faster ai agent responses and low app latency with azure managed redis
description: Low latency and fast response times are key to the performance of AI agents. aiDescription:
Introduction to Azure Managed Redis The event started with an overview of Azure Managed Redis, describing it as a fully managed, enterprise-grade Redis platform deeply integrated into Azure. It's designed for the next wave of distributed and AI applications, covering a range of use cases from caching to real-time analytics and AI workloads.

Session Highlights Azure Managed Redis is highlighted as featuring multi-core support, active-active geo-replication, and up to five nines of availability, which ensures high performance and reliability across global applications. It combines the familiarity of open-source Redis with enterprise-ready features from Redis Enterprise.

Performance and Scalability Enhancements to Azure Managed Redis have yielded significant performance improvements in commonly used Redis commands, making applications faster and more efficient. These advancements are part of ongoing efforts to leverage modern hardware technologies such as SSDs for cost-effective storage solutions, catering to large-scale applications.

AI and Semantic Caching Capabilities The session also delved into the AI capabilities of Azure Managed Redis, particularly emphasizing its role in powering semantic caching and agentic memory for generative AI applications. Semantic caching helps optimize response time and reduce computational overhead by storing and retrieving data based on semantic similarity rather than exact text matches.

Future Developments and Innovations Looking ahead, Azure Managed Redis plans to introduce new services tailored to AI use cases, including a semantic cache service and an agentic memory service. These offerings are designed to enhance the platform's ability to support advanced AI functionalities and improve the developer experience.

Interactive Q&A Session The event concluded with a Q&A session where the attendees engaged with the speakers to clarify doubts and inquire more about specific features. This interactive segment helped the audience understand the practical aspects of migrating to and utilizing Azure Managed Redis in their projects.

The Disconnect Between Development and Security: AI red teaming is highlighted as a manual, resource-intensive task that involves close collaboration between developers and security teams. Launch of AI Red Teaming Agent: The introduction of the AI Red Teaming Agent, an offering from Azure AI Foundry built on Microsoft's open-source framework 'Pirate', was announced. Demonstration and Technical Insights: A live demonstration was provided to showcase how the AI Red Teaming Agent operates and its integration with Azure services. This helps in predicting and mitigating potential security risks in real-world scenarios.

Practical Applications and Benefits: Throughout the discussion, examples of practical applications and benefits were shared, emphasizing how AI red teaming ensures safety and security from development to deployment. description: Join the industry luminaries behind Accelerate and The Phoenix Project for a fireside chat on Agentic DevOps—where AI fuels elite performance and unlocks groundbreaking innovation. Discover how AI is reshaping the developer experience, enhancing collaboration, and sparking fresh perspectives on emerging trends like Vibe Coding. Expect candid insights, bold predictions, and a lively discussion that illuminates the future of DevOps.
aiDescription:
Welcome and Introduction: The session opened with a warm welcome to the audience, emphasizing the focus on how artificial intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing the field of DevOps. The conversation aimed to explore the impact of AI on software development and operational practices.

AI's Role in DevOps: The discussion highlighted that AI is not just about automating routine tasks but fundamentally changing how DevOps functions. It facilitates faster decision-making and problem-solving, enabling teams to focus on more strategic tasks that add value to the business.

Panel Introduction: The panel comprised key figures from the software delivery field, contributing diverse perspectives based on their experiences with implementing AI technologies in DevOps practices. They discussed the evolution of DevOps, integration of AI, and the importance of maintaining a balance between automation and human insight.

Challenges and Predictions: The conversation delved into the challenges faced by organizations in integrating AI within DevOps. It also covered predictions about future developments in the field, emphasizing the need for adaptive strategies that can evolve with technological advancements.

Strategic Implementation: The importance of a strategic approach to implementing AI in DevOps was stressed. The panelists shared insights on achieving successful integration, including the necessity for cultural change within organizations, fostering an environment open to innovation and continuous learning.

Conclusion and Next Steps: The session concluded with a call to action for the attendees to think critically about how AI can be leveratively leveraged within their organizations. The panelists encouraged the audience to experiment with AI tools and techniques, continually adapt to new changes, and prioritize upskilling to stay relevant in the evolving tech landscape.

title: How we power Xbox services at scale and boost developer productivity with C# & .NET Aspire
sortTitle: how we power xbox services at scale and boost developer productivity with c# & .net aspire
description: Come see how the Xbox Live team at Microsoft has transformed their developer productivity by adopting the latest versions of .NET and deeply integrating with .NET Aspire for orchestration, Azure integrations, and insights with OpenTelemetry. In this session you will learn about the core of Xbox architecture and how we build and scale with .NET
aiDescription:
Introduction to Presentation: The session commenced with a general greeting and introduction by the presenter. He provided an overview of his role within the Xbox Services team and set the stage for discussing the utilization of .NET Aspire to enhance productivity in their operations.

Discussion on Xbox Services: The presentation delved into specific services related to Xbox, explaining the architecture and flow of these services, particularly focusing on user login and social interactions within the Xbox network. The presenter emphasized the scale of these services, citing examples of different user scenarios experienced during gameplay and the technical backend involved in managing these services efficiently using Azure and related tools.

Architecture and Integration Aspects: The speaker outlined the underlying architecture, stressing the microservice-based approach and integration with various Azure services such as Event Hub, Cosmos DB, and Blob storage. He elaborated on the technical stack utilized, including C#, REST APIs, and asp.net for service operations along with Otel for telemetry.

Live Demonstration: A considerable portion of the session was dedicated to a live demonstration where the implementation of .NET Aspire was showcased. This included a walkthrough of the setup process in Visual Studio, the configuration of Azure services, and running example services locally to demonstrate the streamlined process and potential productivity gains from using .NET Aspire.

Challenges and Solutions: Highlighting previous challenges such as complex startup scripts and manual configurations, the presenter demonstrated how .NET Aspire simplifies these issues. He showed how it facilitates local testing and development by integrating with Azure services seamlessly, therefore, improving the local development environment significantly.

Conclusion and Implications: The session concluded with a recap of the efficiency and enhanced capabilities demonstrated through the .NET Aspire framework. It emphasized the potential for developers to replicate this setup easily, ensuring a quick start and minimal configuration hassles due to the streamlined processes and integrated services.

title: Secure and govern AI agents and apps with Microsoft Purview
sortTitle: secure and govern ai agents and apps with microsoft purview
description: Join Rohan Kumar, CVP, Microsoft Security, Purview, & Trust, as he details the value of Microsoft Purview, its data security and compliance capabilities, and how Microsoft protects developers by securing and governing AI agents.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Microsoft Security: The backstage interview takes place at Build 2025, where a Microsoft Security employee discusses their role in data security with a focus on Microsoft Purview. The conversation highlights the person's transition from working in data space to specializing in data security, emphasizing the importance of securing sensitive data across platforms.

Overview of Microsoft Purview: Microsoft Purview is spotlighted as a significant tool for managing data security. It involves safeguarding AI apps and agents, ensuring high-value data within enterprises is protected from potential threats like oversharing and leaks. This forms a critical part of Microsoft's broader security strategy, ensuring compliance and privacy across data operations.

Strategic Investments in Security: The discussion touches on strategic investments in three main areas concerning data and AI security. These include agent identity protection with Antra, extending Defender for cloud assets, and utilizing Microsoft Purview for comprehensive data governance and compliance, which is depicted as crucial for enterprise-level data management.

Importance of Data Security in AI: As AI technologies evolve, maintaining robust data security protocols becomes paramount. The person underscores how enterprises face considerable risks related to data leaks and oversharing due to the powerful capabilities of AI. Microsoft addresses these challenges by integrating data security measures directly into AI app development stages, enhancing overall security architecture.

Empowering Developers and Enterprises: The focus also moves to how Microsoft enables developers and enterprises to implement effective security measures easily. Tools like the Purview SDK allow seamless integration of security features into various applications, promoting a developer-friendly environment while ensuring strict compliance and governance is upheld.

Conclusion with a Unified Security Approach: Finally, the conversation concludes on the note of Microsoft's comprehensive security strategy that spans several products and tools. With an emphasis on a unified approach that caters to various aspects of security from network endpoints to AI apps, Microsoft is positioned as a leader in providing advanced security solutions that cater to the dynamic needs of modern enterprises.

speakerNames: Adam Braden, ROHIT KURUP, Abhijat Singh
title: Protecting users, apps, and data on Windows from a high threat world
sortTitle: protecting users, apps, and data on windows from a high threat world
description: Learn how evolving security measures on Windows will impact resource access, identity protection, and malware prevention. As well as gain insights on new tools, APIs, and best practices to safeguard sensitive data. Don't miss this opportunity to stay ahead of digital threats and enhance your app's security. Perfect for developers looking to protect their users and apps.
aiDescription:
Welcome and Agenda: The session began with an introduction by Rohit Kurup, who along with his colleagues, discussed strategies to protect users, apps, and data in Windows against high-threat environments. The session was structured to cover the current regulatory landscape, the significance of app identity for user privacy, administrator protection, and the application of VBS Enclaves SDK for securing applications and data.

Regulatory Landscape: The discussion highlighted the evolving global regulatory framework that impacts industry standards for privacy, security, and compliance. Various international privacy and security laws are being strengthened, adding complexity for businesses operating across borders. Emphasis was placed on the burgeoning regulations surrounding artificial intelligence and the necessity for businesses to adapt to these changes proactively.

App Identity and User Privacy: A significant part of the session was dedicated to understanding how app identity could bolster user privacy. Using app identity helps in aligning with regulatory expectations and building user trust by ensuring that users have granular controls over their data and the apps accessing them. The forthcoming changes in default settings for privacy controls in Windows were also previewed, stressing the importance for app developers to prepare for these updates.

Administrator Protection: Another critical aspect discussed was administrator protection, which aims to mitigate the risks associated with administrative privileges. The introduction of a new security feature in Windows now requires admin users to have just-in-time access to critical resources, thereby reducing the risk of malware attacks and malicious activities.

VBS Enclaves SDK: Towards the end of the session, Adam introduced the VBS Enclaves SDK which provides a secure environment for executing code and handling data within applications. This technology is vital for protecting sensitive applications and data from advanced threats. This part of the session covered technical details on implementing VBS Enclaves to enhance application security.

Conclusion and Call to Action: The session concluded with a call to action for the participants to leverage Microsoft's security and compliance toolkits, stay abreast of new regulations, and proactively implement privacy and security features in their applications. Encouragement was given to use the newly discussed technologies and feedback was solicited to improve future offerings.

Visual Studio Integration and UI Inspection: The integration with Visual Studio allows for a seamless design and coding experience. The convenience of these tools helps developers inspect, modify, and understand the components more efficiently.

Demonstration and Practical Application: The speaker proceeded to demonstrate the discussed tools using a basic Maui app directly within Visual Studio. He emphasized how the tools are not only limited to Visual Studio 17 but are also compatible with other XAML-based languages in .NET, highlighting their versatility and broad application scope.

Extended Tool Capabilities and User Interaction: Further into the presentation, advanced features such as the visual tree were shown, enabling developers to navigate through an app's structure visually. The interaction with these tools appeared user-friendly, potentially reducing the learning curve and encouraging wider adoption among developers.

Conclusion and Encouragement for Adoption: Concluding the presentation, the speaker underscored the importance of staying updated with the latest versions of these tools to maximize productivity. The call to action was clear—embrace modern tooling within Visual Studio to enhance development workflows and achieve better results in software projects.
