title: Accelerating Scientific Discovery with HPC, AI, and Quantum Computing
sortTitle: accelerating scientific discovery with hpc, ai, and quantum computing
description: In this session, discover how the integration of High-Performance Computing (HPC), Artificial Intelligence (AI), and ultimately Quantum Computing can revolutionize research and development in many industries (like chemicals, manufacturing, life sciences and more). Learn how these cutting-edge technologies are going to scale and accelerate R&D efforts that could address some of our world’s toughest challenges.
aiDescription:
Introduction and Platform Overview: The session kicked off with an introduction to the Microsoft Discovery platform. The platform, which integrates AI, quantum computing, and High-Performance Computing (HPC), aims to revolutionize scientific research by facilitating the discovery process across various domains, including chemistry and material science.

Agent-Based System: Microsoft Discovery operates through an advanced, agent-based system. These agents are designed to behave like AI-powered researchers, performing tasks such as data analysis, hypothesis generation, and simulation. This system underscores a holistic approach, ensuring researchers can focus on innovation without getting bogged down by the intricacies of data processing or model training.

Extensibility and Collaboration: A significant feature of the platform is its extensibility, allowing for the incorporation of external tools and models. This flexibility not only enhances the platform's capabilities but also nurtures a collaborative ecosystem amongst scientific researchers. Partners can contribute tools and models, which enrich the platform's resources and utility.

Quantum Computing Integration: The integration of quantum computing is set to amplify the platform's data processing prowess. Quantum technologies are discussed as pivotal in handling complex molecular and chemical data structures which would otherwise be prohibitive with classical computing techniques.

Security and Ethical Considerations: Microsoft emphasizes responsible computing by incorporating robust security measures within the Discovery platform, especially critical as quantum computing could potentially decrypt current encryption standards. Microsoft participates in creating quantum-safe encryption methods to prevent future susceptibilities.

Conclusion and Future Prospects: The session concluded with an emphasis on the scalable nature of the Discovery platform, promising to keep pace with evolving scientific needs and computational technologies. The integration of community-driven tools and models through a flexible architecture ensures that Microsoft Discovery remains adaptive and relevant to a broad spectrum of scientific inquiries.

title: Inside Azure innovations with Mark Russinovich
sortTitle: inside azure innovations with mark russinovich
description: Join Mark Russinovich, CTO, Deputy CISO, Technical Fellow of Microsoft Azure. Mark will take you on a tour of the latest innovations in Azure architecture and explain how Azure enables intelligent, modern, and innovative applications at scale in the cloud, on-premises, and on the edge.
aiDescription:
Session Overview:

The session provided a comprehensive view of Microsoft's innovative efforts across its Azure platform. The focus was on infrastructure innovation, enhancements in Azure capabilities, and the introduction of future technologies.

Infrastructural Innovations:

The advancements in Azure infrastructure were highlighted, especially the development of Azure Boost 2.0. This new iteration facilitates enhanced performance through the offload card, which optimizes server use by offloading storage and network data paths to accelerated hardware, consequently freeing server cores for their primary tasks.

Cloud Confidential Computing:

An explanation was given on cloud confidential computing, aiming to enhance data security by protecting data when it is in use. This involves cryptographic assurances and specialized hardware to ensure data is processed in a secure enclave, thereby safeguarding sensitive information from both external threats and malicious insiders.

AI Innovations:

The session also discussed the use of AI driven by Azure innovations. Emphasis was placed on integrated AI functionalities within the cloud platform, enhancing capabilities such as analytics and machine learning operations directly within Azure.

Future Computing Platforms:

A speculative discussion about future computing platforms rooted in Microsoft Research's work was also presented. This includes exploring new paradigms such as optical computing that could potentially revolutionize processing speeds and energy efficiency by utilizing light for computational tasks.

User Engagement and Demo:

The speaker provided a live demonstration of Azure's capabilities, showcasing real-time computing potential through various Azure-driven applications. This practical display helped in illustrating the discussed innovations and bolstered engagement by providing a tangible look at how these technologies function in real scenarios.

title: Learn How Microsoft secured the Engineering System
sortTitle: learn how microsoft secured the engineering system
description: One of the pillars of Secure Future Initiative is securing the engineering system. We will explore changes made to Azure DevOps and Microsoft's engineering systems to enhance security for Microsoft products and how customers can use them to better secure their own engineering systems as well.
aiDescription:
Welcome and Introduction: The session opened with a welcome to the last breakout session at the Build conference, with a focus on shifting the discussion from AI to discussing security, particularly Microsoft's engineering system security.

Session Focus: The session concentrated on various aspects of security within Microsoft's engineering systems. It covered the importance of security in the engineering process, highlighting Microsoft's strategy to ensure robust security through integrated security guardrails, helping minimize security-related toil for Microsoft engineers.

Security Initiative: A significant portion of the talk was dedicated to discussing the 'Secure Future Initiative,' which is Microsoft's comprehensive approach to enhancing security. This includes a focus on developing software with security built into the design, ensuring secure default settings, and maintaining robust security operations.

Security Practices: The presentation outlined several security best practices and principles applied at Microsoft, such as the need for engineers to consider security at every stage of software development and the integration of security checks throughout the development lifecycle.

Q&A Session: A question and answer session followed where the audience engaged with queries about the implementation of security measures across different Microsoft platforms, including Azure DevOps and GitHub, as well as strategies for managing SSH keys and personal access tokens.

Closing: The session wrapped up with an invitation for further discussion outside the conference room for those interested in more detailed inquiries or follow-up questions on the security protocols and measures at Microsoft.

speakerNames: Patty Chow, Khelan Modi, Hari Krishna Sunder, Rohan Thomas
title: Scale and secure MongoDB-compatible apps with Azure Cosmos DB
sortTitle: scale and secure mongodb-compatible apps with azure cosmos db
description: How do you ensure the benefits of cloud when modernizing apps and data, without wasting time or money? This session will show how to move an existing MongoDB-compatible workload to Azure with zero downtime. Enhance scalability, enterprise security, and performance, including real-time sync via change data capture. The session will also showcase code-heavy demos on the open-source MongoDB-compatible multi-cloud database, DocumentDB, that powers vCore-based Azure Cosmos DB for MongoDB.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Azure Cosmos DB and MongoDB: The webinar focused on demonstrating various features and capabilities of Azure Cosmos DB, particularly in relation to scaling, securing, and migrating MongoDB compatible applications on Azure. There was a strong emphasis on leveraging Azure for enhanced performance and security.

Agenda Overview: Initially, the conversation centered around building open-source solutions on-premise using Azure native tools. The presenters aimed to guide the audience through the process of migrating and scaling applications using Azure Cosmos DB, ultimately leading to building a hybrid solution.

Migration Options: Two primary migration strategies were discussed—offline and online migration. Offline migration is suitable for environments where temporary downtime is permissible, while online migration caters to applications requiring continuous uptime, leveraging real-time data sync.

Hybrid Solution and Global Expansion: As part of the hybrid solution approach, a scenario was described where the application could operate both on-premises and on Azure, ensuring better management of data across different geographical locations. This segment was particularly relevant for businesses looking to expand globally while adhering to regional compliance and data residency requirements.

Demonstrations and Practical Implementations: Several demonstrations were provided showing the setup and configuration processes within Azure, using VS Code for effective management of the databases. These practical insights were aimed at helping attendees understand the operational aspect of managing databases on Azure.

Interactive Q&A Session: The webinar concluded with a Q&A session where attendees could ask specific questions regarding Azure Cosmos DB, MongoDB, and hybrid solutions. This session helped clarify doubts and provided deeper insights into using Azure for database management effectively.

Vector store in Azure Cosmos DB for MongoDB vCore

title: Innovate, deploy, & optimize your apps without infrastructure hassles
sortTitle: innovate, deploy, & optimize your apps without infrastructure hassles
description: Learn more about the latest announcements for autoscaling capabilities, resiliency and high availability, and application monitoring and observability to enhance service-level objectives. Discover how managed cloud services handle maintenance, monitoring, updates, and troubleshooting, allowing you to focus solely on your applications. This session will equip you with the knowledge to efficiently deploy, scale, and monitor your cloud applications.
aiDescription:
Introduction to AI Potential: The session began with an introduction highlighting advancements in AI technology, demonstrating its potential to streamline various tasks and workflows. The emphasis was on AI's ability to tackle complex processes, enhancing productivity and efficiency in different business scenarios.

AI in Workflow Automation: The discussion delved into how AI is integrated within workflow automation tools, providing examples of how tasks that traditionally required manual human intervention are now being automated. This includes data extraction, decision-making processes, and communication tasks within business operations.

AI Agents and Tools: The conversation included a detailed exploration of developing AI agents that can perform autonomous and dynamic tasks based on predefined rules and parameters. Examples included agents capable of processing loan applications by assessing risk profiles and making decisions on approvals.

AI-Powered Decision Making: A significant highlight was the use of AI in enhancing decision-making processes. Agents can analyze extensive data to provide insights and make informed decisions, reducing the dependency on human analysis, which can be time-consuming and prone to errors.

Integration with Existing Systems: It was emphasized that AI tools and agents can be integrated with existing business systems and infrastructures, allowing organizations to leverage AI without overhauling their existing workflows. This integration facilitates a seamless flow of data and enhances the capabilities of current systems.

Future of AI in Business: The session concluded by reflecting on the future implications of AI in business, suggesting that AI will become increasingly fundamental in handling more complex aspects of business operations. It pointed towards a future where AI not only supports but also advances core business strategies.

title: Local AI development with Foundry Local and .NET Aspire
sortTitle: local ai development with foundry local and .net aspire
description: When we start building apps infused with AI it begs the question of how we tackle the local dev experience. Do we provision cloud resources, if so, how do we secure them, manage rate limits and cost? Or do run local models, and how do we set them up? What about vector databases, data ingestion, and all those auxiliary systems? It all sounds complicated, that is until we start leveraging the power of .NET Aspire and see that we can have it as easy as clone repo and dotnet run!
aiDescription:
Overview of Session: The session began with an acknowledgment of the audience's time, emphasizing appreciation for their presence towards the end of a lengthy day. The speaker expressed challenges involving juggling tasks during the session, suggesting a compelling reason for attendees to stay engaged.

Introduction to Foundry Local: The discussion transitioned into an exploration of "Foundry Local" and its applications in managing generative AI models either locally or in the cloud. The presenter fielded questions on whether the audience was currently employing generative AI and whether they preferred local or cloud-based operations. This led to a broader conversation on the benefits and challenges associated with each method.

Advantages of Local Processing: Running generative AI models locally on devices was highlighted as beneficial primarily due to cost savings and data privacy, as it avoids the fees associated with cloud services and keeps data on the device itself. Furthermore, local processing eliminates reliance on internet connectivity and allows complete control over the data and the processing speed, contingent on the device’s capabilities.

Challenges and Solutions in Local AI: Nevertheless, local execution of models comes with its set of challenges, particularly around hardware limitations. The speaker discussed how local models necessitate specific hardware configurations to function correctly, which might not be feasible for all devices, especially in scenarios where a model needs to reach a broad user base with varying devices. "Foundry Local" addresses these issues by facilitating the optimal model delivery to any given device, considering its hardware capabilities.

Technical Demonstration: In the latter part of the session, a technical demonstration was attempted, showcasing how to integrate and manage AI models using Foundry Local in a development environment. This included discussions and code examples on how to request models, manage their compatibility with different device hardware, and ensure they run optimally in varied environments.

Concluding Thoughts: The session concluded by reiterating the significance of managing computational resources efficiently when running AI models locally. The speaker encouraged further discussion and questions off-stage, promoting interactive engagement and practical demonstrations to solidify the audience's understanding of the concepts presented.
location: Arch, 705 Pike, Level 4, Hub, Theater B

title: Build and ship cross-platform apps on-device with Foundry Local
sortTitle: build and ship cross-platform apps on-device with foundry local
description: Join us for a live coding demo to show you how to develop an AI-enabled application on device, in less than 15 mins. In real time you see how to download and install the tools, choose the best model, and develop the application.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Foundry Local: The session began with introductions from the speakers, part of the Foundry local team, who expressed their enthusiasm about presenting. They announced the release of Foundry Local, a developer tool designed for running AI models on-device, which offers benefits in terms of cost, speed, and privacy.

Benefits of Foundry Local: Foundry Local allows users to operate AI models directly on their hardware without needing API keys or subscriptions, making it cost-effective. It leverages the full potential of the user’s hardware resulting in better speed. Additionally, it enhances privacy since operations can be carried out offline, directly on the device.

Platform Compatibility and Models: The tool is compatible across different platforms including Windows and MacOS, and it can run models in Onyx format using Onyx runtime. This versatility ensures a wide range of applications can utilize Foundry Local regardless of the operating system.

Demonstration and Usage: A live demonstration was performed to showcase the installation and functionality of Foundry Local. The demonstration highlighted the simplicity of setting up and running a model on Foundry Local, with the team executing a coding example without Wi-Fi, except for downloading a model. This exemplified the tool’s functionality in offline settings.

Interactive Session and Model Performance: During an interactive part of the session, the audience provided prompts for the AI to generate creative content like poems, showcasing the responsiveness and creativity of the models run by Foundry Local. The session also emphasized the tool’s cross-platform capabilities and the quick results from model processing.

Conclusion and Additional Resources: The session concluded with a summary of key points and a Q&A segment where the speakers provided additional resources and support links for Foundry Local. They reiterated the advantages of using Foundry Local for secure, efficient, and cost-effective AI model execution on various devices.
location: Arch, 705 Pike, Level 4, Hub, Theater B

title: Get Started with AI Dev Gallery in Minutes
sortTitle: get started with ai dev gallery in minutes
description: Want to add AI to your Windows apps without the hassle? AI Dev Gallery is an open-source collection of ready-to-use AI samples that run locally—no cloud dependencies, no complex setup. In this session, we’ll show you how to grab a sample, plug it into your app, and start using AI for text recognition, image processing, and more. Whether you’re building with WinUI, WPF, or beyond, AI Dev Gallery makes local AI easy. Join us and get started in minutes!
aiDescription:
Welcome and Introduction: The session opened in Theater B with a focus on an exciting presentation by two individuals from the Windows Developer platform team. Their talk aimed to introduce attendees to the AI Dev Gallery, which could be set up in just a few minutes.

AI Dev Gallery Overview: The AI Dev Gallery was shown as a user-friendly platform available through the Microsoft Store. It enables users to engage with various local AI capabilities on Windows, featuring over 25 interactive samples ranging from text, image, smart controls, to audio and video applications.

Onyx Source Models and Hugging Face Integration: The gallery includes Onyx open source models which are pre-loaded automatically when downloading the app. Additionally, it offers seamless integration with Hugging Face, allowing users to fetch models directly without leaving the app. This facilitates exploration and usability of cutting-edge AI tools.

Model Flexibility and Handling: Highlighting the adaptability, developers can switch between models and modify execution providers depending on the device capabilities, including CPU and MPU. This ensures optimal performance and usage of available resources without user intervention.

Code Export and Sample Adaptation: An essential feature discussed was the ability to export sample codes from the gallery directly into new Visual Studio projects. This allows easy incorporation of AI capabilities into personal applications, fostering development and customization.

Concluding Notes: The discussions concluded on a high note encouraging attendees to explore the AI Dev Gallery further and contribute to its open-source projects. This platform indeed aligns with ease of use, catering to developers’ needs to integrate AI into their projects efficiently.
location: Arch, 705 Pike, Level 4, Hub, Theater B

title: Build and protect MCPs faster with governance in Azure API Management
sortTitle: build and protect mcps faster with governance in azure api management
description: With the increasing adoption of MCPs, the need for robust management of remote MCPs is intensifying. By treating MCP server endpoints as other backend APIs, organizations can apply familiar governance, security, and operational controls with Azure API Management. Join us as we walk through a practical example of hosting and protecting a remote MCP server behind Azure API Management.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Azure API Management: The session began with discussions on Azure API Management's integration with the Microsoft ecosystem, especially emphasizing its role in enhancing MCP (Microsoft Copilot) capabilities. It serves as an AI gateway and aids in creating secure MCP servers, which are essential for developers using tools like VS Code and AI Foundry.

API Transformation and Security: Azure API Management facilitates the transformation of existing APIs into MCP servers and ensures their security by incorporating built-in authorization and authentication capabilities. This process is streamlined to avoid complex setups and is compliant with Anthropic's official authorization specifications.

Demonstration and Practical Application: A live demonstration was conducted to show how to convert APIs into MCP servers using Azure Portal. This involved importing APIs, setting up API management instances, and configuring operations. The session also covered setting up policies like Validate Jot to enhance security.

Client Integration and Endpoint Security: The newly created MCP servers can be integrated with MCP clients like AI Copilot in VS Code. This integration allows users to directly access and manage the servers via VS Code, leveraging Microsoft's Copilot for streamlined operations and enhanced security through precise control over API permissions.

Real-time Execution and Monitoring: Throughout the session, real-time execution of configured APIs was demonstrated to highlight the seamless interaction between MCP servers and clients. Furthermore, the integration with Azure's monitoring tools like Application Insights allows users to track API performance, response times, and failure rates effectively.

Conclusion and Further Assistance: The session concluded with a summary of the key points discussed, emphasizing the ease of transforming and securing APIs using Azure API Management. Attendees were encouraged to visit the booth for more information or to address any specific queries or needs they might have regarding the Azure API Management setup and usage.

title: Mission: Scalable – Elastic Cloud Serverless Meets Azure OpenAI
sortTitle: mission: scalable – elastic cloud serverless meets azure openai
description: Discover how Elastic Cloud Serverless and Azure OpenAI empower the creation of AI-driven search applications with effortless scalability. This presentation explores Elastic's intuitive serverless architecture and dynamic scaling, integrated with Azure OpenAI to create world class search experiences. Learn how this powerful partnership simplifies deployments and accelerates innovation for modern search, observability, and security workloads.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Elastic Cloud Serverless: The session commenced with an introduction to Elastic Cloud Serverless integration with Azure Open AI, focusing on simplifying technology while maintaining robust control over server operations. The presentation acknowledged the audience's varying needs from high control to simplicity using a unique analogy related to barbeque preparation methods.

Elastic Cloud Serverless Solutions: Various service models were discussed, such as on-premises solutions offering maximum control, and SaaS models providing ease but less flexibility. Elastic Cloud Kubernetes and Elastic Cloud on Azure were highlighted as solutions that balance control with ease of use, optimizing operational efficiency and scalability.

Architectural Advancements: Significant emphasis was placed on the shift from stateful to stateless architectures in Elastic Cloud Serverless. This transition utilizes object storage for resilience and scalability, enhancing performance and reducing costs by optimizing resource usage through intelligent auto-scaling techniques.

Integration and Implementation: Practical implementation was demonstrated through the integration of Elastic Cloud Serverless with Azure Open AI. This illustrated how companies could leverage these solutions to enhance data handling and query capabilities, drastically simplifying the deployment and management of data-intensive applications.

Future Enhancements and Availability: The roadmap for Elastic Cloud Serverless includes expanded cloud and region support, enhanced security features, and cross-project search capabilities. A highlight was the upcoming general availability on Microsoft Azure, making the platform accessible to a broader audience.

Conclusion and Resources: The session concluded with a call to action, encouraging participants to utilize the Elastic Cloud Serverless free trial, available without credit card requirements. Resources such as a dedicated public roadmap and community support channels were provided to assist users in their transition and implementation of Elastic Cloud Serverless solutions.

title: The Agentic Web [Part 1]

description: Join Kevin Scott as he shares insights on the current state of AI, recent advancements, and how to approach a future where helpful agents make AI more useful for developers and users alike. aiDescription:

Deep Research in AI: Scott delves into the concept of 'the capability overhang,' where the current AI capabilities exceed the applications they are being put to. He introduces groundbreaking tools such as Copilot Researcher and ChatGPT, which have been capable of performing complex, creative tasks that were previously unthinkable. These tools, along with enhanced reasoning models, provide a glimpse into the extraordinary potential AI holds for the near future.

Future Predictions and the Agentic Web: Looking ahead, Scott predicts continuing improvements in AI reasoning capabilities and user interfaces. He suggests that the broader adoption of AI will not only enhance existing applications but also enable users to perform increasingly complex tasks more efficiently. The focus also shifts to ensuring AI systems are accessible and utilitarian across various professional fields without substantial barriers.

Enhancements and Community Involvement: He encourages a community-driven approach to solving AI challenges, such as memory integration and better handling of tasks. Open-source contributions and a unified effort from the developer community could accelerate resolving these challenges, making AI more robust and capable. Scott stresses the importance of an open and shared development environment where innovations are freely accessible and not confined within proprietary boundaries.

Conclusion and Encouragement: In his concluding remarks, Scott reiterates his optimism and excitement about the future of AI. He encourages the audience to harness the full potential of the agentic web to create innovative solutions that address pressing problems. He hopes that the shared tools and knowledge will inspire attendees to explore AI's possibilities and contribute to its evolution in the coming years.

aiDescription:

Enhanced Dashboard Capabilities:

The Copilot dashboard now features comprehensive metrics addressing adoption rates, daily and weekly usage, and overall impact. These insights are crucial for organizations to monitor and optimize their use of the allocated Copilot licenses effectively.

Real-Time Data and Historical Trends:

The dashboard draws real-time data directly from GitHub for billing and licensing information, presenting it alongside historical usage trends. This combination helps organizations make informed decisions about license management based on actual usage and detected patterns over time.

License Utilization Analysis:

The discussion highlighted concerns about paying for unused licenses. For example, if a large organization has many unutilized licenses, they can reconsider their needs based on these insights. Furthermore, understanding why certain licenses remain unused could indicate a lack of alignment with developers' needs or preferences.

Productivity and Code Quality Insights:

The tool not only tracks quantitative data like license usage but also delves into qualitative aspects such as how these tools impact developer productivity and code quality. The insights drawn can pinpoint inefficiencies or highlight successful strategies that could be replicated across teams or projects.

Adoption Challenges and Solutions:

Despite the benefits, some challenges with Copilot adoption and effectiveness were noted, particularly concerning its alignment with specific coding languages or projects. For organizations facing these issues, customized or fine-tuned models of Copilot might offer a solution by better meeting the developers' needs in specific contexts.

**Terraform and AVM Integration:** Kareem from Hashicorp shared updates on the new features in Terraform, showing how it integrates effectively with Azure Verified Models to optimize resource deployment and management on Azure platforms. **Resource Management and Security:** Throughout the session, emphasis was placed on managing resources effectively using Azure modules and improving security through standardized processes and adherence to Azure’s well-architected framework. Issues like code rationalization, decoupling, and reduction of redundancy were discussed to improve deployment efficiency and cloud resource management.

**Engagement and Resources:** The speakers encouraged the audience to engage with the available resources and explore the robust capabilities of Azure Verified Modules and Azure Landing Zones for improving their cloud infrastructure setups. title: Reflections on 25 years of writing secure code
sortTitle: reflections on 25 years of writing secure code
description: Michael Howard - author of the original secure coding book back in 2003 and now a senior director in the Microsoft Red Team - reflects on 25 years of secure code, how the practice has developed and current challenges in secure development. Be prepared for an end-to-end secure coding journey from Bill Gates anecdotes and tips for how you in 2025 can code securely to meet today's security threats.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Secure Code Evolution: The lecture opens with a retrospective on the evolution of secure coding practices over 25 years, originating from the release of a significant book on secure coding. The speaker, a co-author, narrates the journey, emphasizing the persistent relevance and the continuous learning in the field of security. He underscores the impact of this realm, touching upon how past experiences and stories from early coding days shape current security perspectives and practices.

Transitioning to Modern Programming Languages: The speaker discusses the historical context and the modern transition from traditional programming languages like C to more secure, robust alternatives like Rust. This shift is advocated due to the integral memory safety and security features that newer languages offer, marking a significant evolution from older languages known for their susceptibility to security vulnerabilities due to unsafe memory practices.

Importance of Input Validation: Throughout the talk, the importance of input validation is stressed as a perpetual issue. The speaker revisits themes from the book "Writing Secure Code," highlighting that vulnerabilities often stem from improper validation mechanisms. This point serves as a both a historical reminder and a current challenge, indicating that despite advancements, input validation remains a fundamental concern that developers must address.

Demonstrations of Security Tools: Practical demonstrations of modern security tools like GitHub’s CodeQL and its functionalities are covered. These tools are shown to aid in static code analysis, allowing for automated detection and even remediation suggestions for security vulnerabilities within codebases. This reflects the advancements in automated security tools that aid developers in maintaining code integrity against potential security threats.

Advocating Comprehensive Security Practices: Towards the end of the session, there's an emphasis on adopting a holistic approach to security. The speaker encourages not only securing the code but also implementing robust security practices throughout the development lifecycle. This includes regular updates, patch management, and the use of modern tools and methodologies to anticipate and mitigate vulnerabilities effectively.

Conclusion and Call to Action: The session concludes with a powerful call to action for embracing modern programming practices, continuous learning, and the integration of advanced tools to fortify security. Attendees are encouraged to leverage both historical knowledge and modern tools to craft secure applications, embodying the principle that effective security is adaptive and ever-evolving.

aiDescription:

Testing and Validation: Once the connector is created, the next phase involves testing and validating its functionality within the Copilot ecosystem. **Developments in WebVTT and AI Tools:** The advancements in WebVTT technology coupled with sophisticated AI tools have enabled developers to enrich user interactions and experiences significantly. title: Lightning-fast analytical queries on massive datasets with ClickHouse
sortTitle: lightning-fast analytical queries on massive datasets with clickhouse
description: Extracting real-time insights from massive datasets is a growing challenge. ClickHouse combines columnar storage and best-in-class compression to produce unparalleled query speed at any scale. Learn how companies like Microsoft, Sony, eBay, and Lyft leverage ClickHouse for applications ranging from user-facing dashboards to ad-hoc analyses over petabytes of data.
aiDescription:
Introduction to ClickHouse: The session begins with an introduction to ClickHouse, an open-source OLAP database engineered for performing lightning-fast analytical queries on massive datasets. The speaker outlines the primary function of ClickHouse, its inception in 2009, and its journey towards becoming open-sourced in 2016. The platform’s capabilities include handling real-time analytical operations and integrating seamlessly with tools like Power BI for business-critical reporting.

Performance and Scalability: ClickHouse is highlighted for its exceptional performance in handling large volumes of data with amazing compression ratios. Architecturally, it is designed to scale flexibly through features like distributed queries and multi-master replication, which support extensive data operations across different regions.

Commercial Success and Community: The discussion proceeds to cover the significant milestones for ClickHouse as a company, including raising substantial funding and achieving a high rank on DB-Engines. The adoption of ClickHouse by tech giants like Microsoft and Cloudflare is discussed, showcasing its robustness in handling immensely high data traffic and speeding up data ingestion significantly.

Use Cases and Integrations: Real-time analytics, including scenarios like high-user concurrency and mission-critical applications, are identified as popular use cases. The talk elaborates on how ClickHouse supports SQL-based observability becoming increasingly popular for monitoring and event logging. Its integration capabilities with various front-end and data ingestion tools are also mentioned, enhancing its adaptability in diverse tech ecosystems.

Cost Efficiency and Management: Addressing cost management, ClickHouse offers an efficient billing model based on resource usage (CPU, memory, storage) rather than per-query charges, which provides predictable costs and better control over budgeting. The cloud version of ClickHouse offers a managed, serverless experience, further simplifying operations for users by allowing them to pay only for the resources they actively use.

Conclusion and Future Directions: The session concludes with insights into future enhancements, focusing on improvements in cloud offerings and ongoing commitments to security and performance. The speaker invites attendees to explore further by visiting their website, suggesting a comprehensive understanding of ClickHouse’s capabilities through additional resources and community contributions.

**Capabilities of GitHub Copilot for Azure:** The talk then presents a demonstration showing how GitHub Copilot for Azure simplifies complex tasks by guiding users through automation scripts within the portal. Copilot assists in creating and managing Azure resources, such as storage accounts, by providing syntactically correct command suggestions and automating resource handling via Azure CLI or PowerShell scripts.

**Conclusion and Further Exploration:** The session concludes by emphasizing the potential of GitHub Copilot to revolutionize Azure operations via artificial intelligence, making script generation and resource management more accessible and less error-prone. title: Unlock developer agility with a well governed environment
sortTitle: unlock developer agility with a well governed environment
description: In a world where app and env requirements are ever changing, maintaining control can be a moving target. Come learn how to empower your developers to achieve more, without compromising on security, compliance, or operational best practices through Azure Governance products. In this session we'll be discussing newly released features within Azure Policy, dive deep into Policy as code, and announce a new grouping construct called Service groups designed to optimize cross subscription management.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Service Groups: The presentation introduced the concept of Service Groups, a new feature in Azure. This new resource container, suitable for various scenarios, supports flexible aggregation of information from across a tenant. It doesn't replace but works parallel to existing structures like management groups and subscriptions. Service groups are particularly handy as they provide hierarchy up to 10 levels deep and allow for flexible membership, meaning a resource can belong to multiple service groups.

Features and Benefits: Service Groups offer a more granular view of resources, making it suitable for versatile scenarios like viewing application hierarchies or aggregating data for cost analysis. They support all resources, resource groups, and subscriptions, and each of these can be part of more than one service group. This flexibility is essential for obtaining detailed and customized views of the Azure environment.

Integration and Use Cases: In the demo, the presenter showcased how Service Groups could be used to model an application, illustrating it with a claims application example. This example demonstrated creating a new service group, adding necessary resources, and then using the Azure Monitor Health model to link and monitor the health of the application seamlessly.

Privacy and Security: Being least privileged by nature, Service Groups ensure that they can be safely used without altering existing permission structures. They do not support policy or RBAC inheritance, which means they are purely organizational and do not affect the deployment scope. This feature ensures that Service Groups can be used broadly without unintentional privilege escalation.

Future Developments and Accessibility: The feature is in public preview, accessible for testing to those who sign up. Future integrations will include richer health models and possibly more sophisticated monitoring tools, increasing the utility of Service Groups in managing complex environments.

Open Discussion and Feedback: The session concluded with an open discussion, highlighting community interest and queries about integrating policy enforcement and management more seamlessly across Azure environments. The feedback and interactions point towards a growing interest in more refined governance tools within Azure.

description: When building SaaS architectures, development teams must design for requirements around tenan isolation, customer onboarding/offboarding, and key principles scalability, security, and resiliency. The Azure Well-Architected SaaS guide provides expert strategies for reliable solutions on Azure. In this session, we will walkthrough an end-to-end SaaS microservice design, covering design areas like compute hosting, network & data isolation, and DevOps, offering key recommendations and considerations.
aiDescription:
Introduction and SAS Discussion: The presentation begins with a light-hearted introduction by Robbie Milsap from the Microsoft Learn team. Despite his humorous start about having eaten Rice Krispie sheets, he segues into a deeper discussion on SAS and the Well-Architected Framework. The key pillars of the framework - performance, efficiency, security, cost optimization, and operational excellence - are outlined for the audience.

Workload Definitions: Robbie elaborates on workloads, defining them as sets of resources that deliver business outcomes. He indicates that workloads can include diverse resources like databases and code. The focus is specifically on a SAS workload under the Well Architected Framework (WAF), which he has been involved with for several years.

This is where bold ideas become prototypes—and developers help define what’s next.
aiDescription:
Azure AI Foundry Labs: The labs provide a curated feed from the research community, focusing on advancements relevant to the developer community.
Multimodal Foundation Models: These models offer human-like reasoning, enabling agents to reason under uncertainty.
Microsoft Research: Originated from a memo by Bill Gates and Nathan Myrtle in the early 90s, it has grown into a global ecosystem of labs and locations.
Phi Model Series: Started as a small research project, it focuses on creating intelligence in smaller packages using carefully curated data.
Microsoft Discovery Platform: A new platform announced to accelerate scientific discovery across various fields like physics, biology, and chemistry.
Omniparser V2: Uses computer vision to turn an LLM into a computer agent, enabling interaction with screen elements.
Magenta One: A specialized agent team designed to perform general tasks across different domains, using a task ledger to track progress.
Magenta Qi: A human-centered computer agent that collaborates with users, ensuring safety and security through action guards and transparency layers.
Autogen: A library enabling multi-agent collaboration, decomposing complex problems into smaller pieces and composing them to achieve final results.
Foundry Labs: Provides access to early-stage research and innovations, allowing developers to prototype and integrate models into their workflows.

Misconceptions about AI: The session addresses several misconceptions about AI, such as it being just autocomplete on steroids, taking longer to explain than to do it oneself, writing buggy code, and being unnecessary for experienced developers.

Inferencing and Model Deployment:

Interactive Elements:

Conclusion:

speakerNames: Scott Guthrie, Charles Lamanna, Jay Parikh
title: Unpacking the tech
sortTitle: unpacking the tech
description: Jay Parikh, Charles Lamanna, Scott Guthrie and friends will dig deeper into the news announced on Day 1, with a focus on the role developers play in helping enterprises successfully embrace AI and digital transformation, the latest in Copilot, Azure, GitHub, and Windows AI innovations, and the significance of trust, security, and governance in all we do.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Microsoft 365 Copilot: At the Microsoft Build Developer Conference, the new Microsoft 365 Copilot was introduced. This AI-powered tool integrates seamlessly with Microsoft 365 apps, enhancing user productivity and creativity. Microsoft 365 Copilot works alongside users, providing assistance in content creation, data analysis, and much more, directly within the user's workflow.

Capabilities and Features: Microsoft 365 Copilot is designed to perform various tasks such as summarizing emails, drafting stories, analyzing data, and preparing presentations. It leverages powerful AI models to offer suggestions and create content, significantly reducing the time and effort users spend on these tasks.

Demonstration and Use Cases: During the conference, several demonstrations showcased how Microsoft 365 Copilot operates within different applications like Word, Excel, and Outlook. For instance, in Word, Copilot helped create a document from scratch by fetching relevant information and drafting content aligned with the user's requests.

Integration Across Microsoft 365: Copilot is not limited to a single application but is integrated across various Microsoft 365 apps. This integration allows it to pull data from different sources, ensuring consistency and richness in the content it helps generate. Whether the user is working on a text document, a spreadsheet, or preparing for a meeting, Copilot provides real-time assistance.

Feedback and Iterations: Microsoft 365 Copilot supports iterative feedback, where users can refine the results by providing commands. For example, if the initial output needs adjustment, the user can instruct Copilot to modify the tone, style, or details, and the tool will adapt accordingly.

Future Developments: Microsoft plans to continue evolving the capabilities of Microsoft 365 Copilot, with future updates aimed at enhancing its integration and expanding its use cases. This ongoing development underscores Microsoft's commitment to leveraging AI to transform user experiences and productivity within their suite of tools.

title: Architecting highly resilient applications in Azure: A technical guide for developers
sortTitle: architecting highly resilient applications in azure: a technical guide for developers
description: Dive into the technical strategies and architectural patterns essential for designing applications that achieve zero downtime, zero data loss, and seamless scalability on Azure. This session will provide developers with hands-on guidance to implement key resilience patterns such as Active-Active replication, Saga transactions, and Global Secondary Indexing. Learn how these patterns are operationalized in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), Azure Cosmos DB, and other critical Azure services. Gain actionable insights on automating deployments, monitoring distributed systems, and optimizing for performance. By the end of this session, developers will have the tools to architect and build robust, fault-tolerant systems tailored to business-critical demands.
aiDescription:
Introduction and Overview: Justine Kochi, a program manager from the Azure Cosmos DB team, begins the session by expressing excitement about discussing the architecture of highly resilient applications within Azure, specifically using an e-commerce website example.

Application Structure: Justine explains that their focus is on architecting a robust application for an e-commerce platform. The architecture leverages Azure Web Apps for the frontend, microservices deployed on Azure Kubernetes Service, and Azure Cosmos DB — a globally distributed database service that scales seamlessly with the application.

User Interaction and Database Utilization: The conversation shifts toward user interaction flow within the website, emphasizing the significant phases like browsing products and checking out. Justine details the backend processing using Azure's infrastructures, such as querying the database for product availability and precise execution of user activities like payments and order confirmations.

Resiliency Tips and Strategies: The discussion dives deeper into strategies for maintaining high resilience and performance. Justine talks about the importance of optimizing operational latency and database interactions, focusing on how to handle failures and ensure efficient data processing and user transactions.

title: Automating Secure and Scalable AI Deployments on Azure with HashiCorp
sortTitle: automating secure and scalable ai deployments on azure with hashicorp
description: Deploying and managing AI workloads on Azure requires a scalable, secure, and efficient approach. This demo shows how the latest Terraform features automate infrastructure provisioning for Microsoft Azure, enabling seamless data integration and AI-driven insights. aiDescription:
Welcome and Context: The session, led by David Wright, focuses on automating secure and scalable AI deployments on Azure using HashiCorp tools. Despite the potential of AI technologies like machine learning and deep learning, a significant number of AI projects fail, with reports indicating that many do not progress beyond the pilot stage.

Key Challenges in AI Projects: The presentation highlights three primary challenges encountered in AI projects: building foundational structures quickly, navigating new security challenges, and scaling solutions across enterprises. These challenges often lead to project failures due to a misalignment with the infrastructure focus rather than business outcomes, which executives prioritize for enhancing customer experiences.

HashiCorp's Role: HashiCorp provides a toolkit designed to address these foundational and security challenges in AI deployment. The tools include capabilities for establishing secure, scalable foundations and managing new data security challenges, thus facilitating the enterprise-wide scaling of AI solutions. The focus on workflows rather than just tools allows for better integration across platforms and leveraging of the partner ecosystem.

Security Paradigm and Challenges: New security challenges are emphasized, such as ensuring perimeter security, maintaining zero trust, and managing data usage. Specific tactics attackers use, such as model poisoning and prompt injection, are discussed to illustrate the security threats AI deployments face. Solutions include implementing stricter access controls and encrypting sensitive data to mitigate risks.

Vault and Terraform Solutions: The use of HashiCorp Vault and Terraform is discussed extensively. Vault helps manage authentication and data encryption, ensuring secure and controlled access to information. Terraform modules facilitate infrastructure deployment, promoting quick and secure setup of AI ecosystems in compliance with best practices and security standards.

Demonstration and Conclusion: A demonstration shows how HashiCorp tools effectively address challenges of deploying and securing AI systems. The combination of Azure and HashiCorp tools enables enterprises to build robust AI solutions, focusing on quick deployment, security, and scalability, thus improving the chances of project progression beyond pilot stages.

Explore how the Intel AI PC Dev Kit, powered by Intel Core Ultra processors with integrated NPUs, accelerates AI workloads and integrates with Windows Copilot Runtime. Gain hands-on experience building AI-driven apps, leveraging Intel hardware and Microsoft’s AI ecosystem. title: Your API is not an MCP
sortTitle: your api is not an mcp
description: A lot of companies have jumped on the MCP bandwagon by quickly auto-generating their MCP servers from their APIs. This isn’t right as MCP servers are made to be consumed by LLMs and not by humans. Join us to better understand why MCPs have to be thought of carefully. Experience examples of tools that make sense for agents but not for humans, and why you should be careful designing your MCP set of tools and resources.
aiDescription:
Introduction and Overview: The speaker begins by introducing themselves and their affiliation with Neon, a serverless Postgres provider. The main topic discussed is MCP (Machine Communication Protocol) servers, focusing on the correct approaches to building these and common pitfalls to avoid.

Understanding MCP and Its Adoption: MCP is a new protocol developed by Anthropic to facilitate the interaction between machine learning models (LLMs) and real-world applications. Despite being relatively new (around 6-7 months old from the time of the discussion), MCP is gaining significant adoption, indicated by its integration into major platforms like Windows Copilot and Google Gemini.

MCP Server Functions and Importance: The core functionality of MCP servers is to expose tools, resources, and prompts to LLMs, helping them to interact effectively with applications. The speaker emphasizes the importance of 'tools' in MCP servers, which can be considered as actions that the LLM might execute, such as database operations in Neon's context.

Building Effective MCP Servers: Challenges in building MCP servers include managing the complexity and ensuring they cater effectively to LLMs' operational modalities. The speaker highlights the inadequacy of merely auto-generating MCP servers from existing API specifications, advocating for a tailored approach that considers the specific needs and capabilities of LLMs.

Practical Demonstration and Use Cases: A practical example provided involves Neon’s use of MCP servers to handle database operations, illustrating how an LLM uses the MCP server to perform tasks like database migrations. This example underscores the importance of designing MCP servers that not only meet technical specifications but also align with practical, real-world use cases.

Conclusion and Recommendations: Concluding the discussion, the speaker advises against auto-generating MCP servers without modifications. Instead, they recommend a hybrid approach where an MCP server is initially auto-generated then refined to ensure it provides just the essential tools with clear, concise descriptions and purpose-built functionalities for LLMs.
location: Arch, 705 Pike, Level 4, Hub, Theater A

Use Cases and Implementation: The speaker mentions multiple potential uses of these agents, including market research, competitive analysis, and personal assistance for tasks like booking restaurants or fetching detailed information. title: Build, Deploy, & Use Your First Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server
sortTitle: build, deploy, & use your first model context protocol (mcp) server
description: What is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server? Why should you consider building one? Why should you consider using them? Join us for this quick session on all things MCP where we will build our very first server in VS Code, test it in VS Code, and even deploy it so others can use it!
aiDescription:
Introduction to Model Context Protocol: The discussion opens with an introduction to the Model Context Protocol (MCP), which is designed to provide AI models and tools with enhanced context and the ability to perform actions effectively. The protocol aims to integrate real-time data and relevant tools across various platforms and services, ensuring that businesses can operate efficiently regardless of the tools they use.

Functionality and Utility: MCP is likened to a universal adapter for AI applications, facilitating seamless connections between AI tools and essential data sources without the need for custom-built interfaces. This analogy underscores MCP's role in enhancing interoperability and streamlining processes within the AI ecosystem.

Core Components: The webinar details the three core components of MCP—the hosts, protocol clients, and servers. Hosts such as Visual Studio or VS Code request access to data via MCP; protocol clients maintain direct connections with servers and can sometimes function as hosts; and servers are light programs that manage data capabilities through MCP.

Implementation and Practical Uses: Several practical applications and implementations of MCP are explored, demonstrating its versatility. Examples include connecting directly to databases, integrating with GitHub for project management, and automating tests with tools like Playwright. These examples illustrate how MCP can be tailored to meet diverse operational needs in real-world scenarios.

Security and Authentication: A significant portion of the discussion addresses the security aspects of MCP, particularly authentication processes necessary for accessing protected APIs and services. The protocol supports standard authentication methods (like OAuth 2.1), which allows users to integrate MCP with existing security frameworks effortlessly, thereby safeguarding sensitive data and operations.

Development and Customization: The dialogue concludes with insights into the development of custom MCP servers and the use of SDKs across various programming languages. This flexibility allows developers to create tailored solutions that meet specific business requirements while ensuring seamless integration and high security. Links to further resources and sessions for hands-on experience are provided, encouraging participants to explore MCP's capabilities in depth.
location: Arch, 705 Pike, Level 4, Hub, Theater A

title: VS Code, Live!
sortTitle: vs code, live!
description:
We're bringing VS Code, Live! for the first time ever, in person-- and you’re on the guest list.
Join us for a session filled with interactive demos, dev sneak peeks, and a behind-the-scenes look at all the latest announcements.
No slides. Just code, conversation, and good vibes, and a few surprises we promise you’ll want to see.
This is VS Code, Live!
aiDescription:
Introduction to VS Code Live: The session begins with a welcome to the VS Code Live event, hosted by Burke and Jason Langstorf from Code TV.
Contestants Introduction: Four contestants are introduced for a live coding challenge: Bridget Murtaugh, Chris Redington, Pierce Boggin, and Harold Kirchner.
Coding Challenge: The contestants are tasked with building a game in 30 minutes. The options include Flappy Bird, Snack, Frogger, and Doodle Jump. Frogger is chosen by the audience.
Contestants' Progress: Each contestant uses different tools and strategies to build their version of Frogger. Bridget uses agent mode powered by GPT-4/1, Chris uses a dual strategy with VS Code and GitHub Copilot, Pierce uses Babylon JS for a 3D Frogger, and Harold uses Veet and three JS.
Agent Mode in VS Code: The session explains the different modes in VS Code: ask mode, edit mode, and agent mode. Agent mode is highlighted for its ability to iterate and reason through tasks.
Open Source Announcement: An announcement is made about Visual Studio Code and GitHub Copilot's AI features being open-sourced, allowing for community contributions and faster development.
MCP (Model Context Protocol): MCP is introduced as a protocol that allows agents to interact with various tools and services, enhancing the capabilities of VS Code.
Live Demonstration: A live demonstration shows how agent mode can be used to build a voting application for the Frogger game contest.
Contestants' Final Presentations: Each contestant presents their final version of the Frogger game, showcasing different features and creative twists.
Winner Announcement: The winner is determined by audience applause, with Chris Redington's GitHub-themed Frogger game taking the win.

title: What's Next in C#
sortTitle: what's next in c#
description: Join Mads and Dustin on a demo-filled tour through upcoming features in C# 14 and beyond. Dictionary expressions, new kinds of extension members and field access in auto-properties are some of the ways that C# keeps making your code clearer, cleaner and more expressive.
aiDescription:
**Introduction:** In the session, further developments and enhancements to the C# language were introduced, including new techniques for handling complex operations more efficiently and making the code cleaner.

**Extension Members:** The focus was on the implementation of extension members in C#. This new feature allows for more flexible and powerful code design, enabling developers to add methods, properties, or other members to classes externally. This can revolutionize how developers manage large codebases and enhance code reusability and organization.

**Operator Overloading and Compound Operators:** Discussion moved towards enhancing operator overloading in C#, including the possibility of defining custom behaviors for compound operators like += or -=. This addition could lead to more nuanced control over how objects handle operations, potentially leading to performance optimizations in data-intensive applications.

**Dictionary Expressions:** A new proposal was discussed for dictionary expressions, which seek to streamline the way dictionaries are manipulated and accessed in C#. By treating dictionaries more like collections, the new expressions would simplify dictionary initialization and usage, fostering more readable and concise code.

**Performance Considerations:** The speakers addressed the balance between adding high-level language features and maintaining the execution performance of C#. They highlighted ongoing efforts to optimize language constructs like collection expressions and pattern matching to ensure they do not adversely affect runtime performance.

**Q&A and Discussion:** The session concluded with a Q&A segment where attendees asked about the roadmap for C# language features, specifically around tuple operations and other potential data structures. The discussion underscored the community's interest in both simplifying code syntax and enhancing the language's capability to handle more sophisticated programming scenarios efficiently.

Overall, the discussion indicated a strong push towards making C# more versatile and efficient, ensuring it remains competitive and capable of addressing modern software development challenges.

title: Orchestrate distributed AI Agents with Azure Functions
sortTitle: orchestrate distributed ai agents with azure functions
description: Explore how AI Agent patterns align with distributed app patterns. Learn the unique value of durable functions in Azure Functions for AI Agent development. Address challenges with open-ended agents and the importance of determinism. Discover how agentic workflows with predefined code paths can mitigate these challenges. See how Microsoft teams have developed products using these technologies and understand practical applications of durable functions for building serverless AI Agents.
aiDescription:
Introduction to AI Agents: Chris and Nick from Orchestrate, a group focused on distributed AI and Azure Functions, introduce their roles in software architecture and product management. They discuss orchestrating distributed AI agents using Azure Functions, aiming to provide context, categorize multi-agent frameworks, and describe agent loops before diving into specific demos and codes.

Agent Interaction and Workflow Types: They highlight the distinction between self-directed agent loops, commonly seen in open-source frameworks, and deterministic agent workflows ideal for repeatable business processes. These workflows facilitate precise control over orchestration and agent involvement, enhancing predictability in process outputs.

Use of Azure Durable Functions: Azure Durable Functions are recommended as a powerful tool for orchestrating agents due to their code-based control, allowing developers to utilize familiar programming constructs like loops and conditions within an environment that supports popular programming languages.

Efficiency and Reliability: The discussion includes the durability and efficiency advantages of using Durable Functions, such as resuming paused workflows without redundancy and scaling resources based on demand, which can significantly reduce operational costs while maintaining robustness against failures.

Demonstration of Azure Durable Functions: Nick demonstrates the potential of Azure Durable Functions with a travel planning application that processes user input to create and manage a trip itinerary. This example showcases the practical application of deterministic workflows and the integration of multiple AI agents to produce a reliable and repeatable process for travel planning.

Capabilities and Monitoring: The durable task scheduler within Azure Functions provides enhanced monitoring and management capabilities, allowing developers to observe and control workflow progress. This session showcases the application's orchestration in real-time, emphasizing the visibility and control developers have over complex workflows and agent interactions.
location: Arch, 705 Pike, Level 4, Hub, Theater A
