Azure Functions integrates with AI Foundry Agent service

speakerNames: Stefanie Lemon, Matt Reat, Max Uritsky
title: Under the Hood: Optimizing Azure applications for performance and efficiency
sortTitle: under the hood: optimizing azure applications for performance and efficiency
description: Discover how to develop cost-effective, scalable, and high-performance applications using the latest features and internal options available in Azure Compute services.  During the session, we'll discuss different CPU architectures that Azure provides, application patterns that are optimized for Intel, AMD, or ARM64, and performance benchmarking tooling.  We will also go deeper into OS-level recommendations, including host networking settings (Microsoft Azure Network Adapter), OS settings and images, and host storage options like Azure Boost SSD.
aiDescription:
Introduction to the Session: This session provides an overview of Azure architecture enhancements, primarily focused on Azure Boost, aiming to optimize compute infrastructure. The discussion delves into the technical details of Azure Boost and its implications for customer workloads, highlighting Microsoft's ongoing commitment to infrastructure innovation.

Understanding Azure Boost: Azure Boost constitutes a significant change in Azure's architecture, likening the process to retrofitting a plane while in flight. This analogy emphasizes the complexity and continues innovation despite ongoing operations. Azure Boost involves extensive rewriting and enhancement, ensuring high availability and robustness, which are critical as cloud infrastructures evolve.

Customer-Centric Enhancements: Azure Boost is designed with customer needs in mind. By optimizing compute infrastructure, Azure Boost significantly enhances performance across various parameters including processing speeds, connectivity, and storage. These advancements not only improve efficiency but also reduce latency, which is crucial for applications requiring real-time processing such as gaming and financial services.

Benefits of Azure Boost: The integration of Azure Boost across Microsoft's cloud services enables higher throughput and better resource management, simplifying operations and maintenance. These improvements are crucial for ensuring customer applications run smoothly without interruption, maintaining Microsoft's competitive edge in the cloud services market.

Technical Insights & Demos: Detailed discussions on the technical aspects of Azure Boost, including network optimizations and storage enhancements, offer deep insights into its operational mechanics. Demos provide practical examples of how Azure Boost improves performance and scalability, illustrating its impact on real-world applications.

Future Prospects: Looking ahead, Microsoft plans to continue developing and expanding Azure Boost capabilities. The session outlines future innovations, further enhancing Azure's infrastructure to better serve diverse customer needs, emphasizing agility, and performance in cloud services.

title: SQL Server 2025: The Database Developer Reimagined
sortTitle: sql server 2025: the database developer reimagined
description: In this session, you will get a deep dive into how SQL Server 2025 brings new features to redefine the database developer experience. This includes vector search with AI models, a new JSON type and index, RegEx and other T-SQL enhancements, Change Event Streaming (CES), and built-in REST API support. Developers can deploy databases on Windows, Linux, containers, k8s, or Azure SQL VM. Copilot assistance is available through SSMS and GitHub. Come see the new path forward for developers using SQL.
aiDescription:
**Introduction to SQL Server 2025:** The presentation at the Microsoft Build conference introduced several enhancements and features being introduced in SQL Server 2025. Its focus was primarily on the advancements in AI integrations and developer-oriented features aimed at refining the user experience and expanding the utility of SQL Server across various applications.

**AI and Model Integration:** A significant portion of the conference was dedicated to showcasing the AI capabilities of SQL Server 2025. The discussion highlighted the flexibility SQL Server 2025 offers in terms of AI integration, allowing users to choose from a wide range of models and connect seamlessly with both on-premises and cloud-based AI services. This provides users the freedom to leverage AI functionalities tailored to their specific needs and environments.

**Advanced Features for Developers:** For developers, SQL Server 2025 promises a plethora of new tools and features, including the ability to handle JSON data natively, enhanced locking mechanisms to prevent common concurrency issues, and optimized query performance. These features are designed to streamline development processes and improve efficiency and performance of database operations.

**Event-Driven Architecture:** Change Event Streaming was highlighted as a key feature, facilitating real-time data processing and integration with other services like Azure Event Hub. This feature is designed to support modern, event-driven applications and improve scalability and responsiveness of systems that rely on SQL Server for data management.

**Security and Scalability Enhancements:** The discussion also touched upon improvements in security and scalability within SQL Server 2025. These enhancements aim to provide more robust data protection measures and a more scalable database environment, which are critical for enterprises handling large volumes of sensitive data.

**Access and Documentation Resources:** Attendees were encouraged to explore SQL Server 2025 further through various resources. Links to downloadable content, detailed documentation, and development tools were provided to help users familiarize themselves with the new features and start integrating them into their applications. The presentation concluded with an invitation to provide feedback and participate in further testing and refinement of SQL Server 2025.

title: The Agentic Web: A conversation with Jay Parikh [Part 2]
sortTitle: the agentic web: a conversation with jay parikh [part 2]
description: Join Jay Parikh and Frank Shaw for a conversation about Microsoft’s vision for the future of software development with AI and how AI agents will enhance user experiences. This is part 2 of a pre-Build discussion that took place on Sunday, May 18. See part 1 here: https://aka.ms/Build2025/KEY030
aiDescription:
Introduction to Build Conference: The discussion is set in the context of Microsoft's Build conference, where various speakers, including a newly joined Microsoft member, discuss their experiences and anticipations. The conversation opens with a casual exchange about attendees' familiarity with the conference and their current roles within Microsoft.

New Role Adjustments: A speaker shares his journey since joining Microsoft, describing the challenging pace and the depth of learning required to get up to speed with the organization’s diverse projects. This adaptation involves understanding the vast operations at Microsoft and organizing his team amidst these dynamics. The conversation reflects a mix of excitement and the overwhelming nature of the transition.

Innovation at Microsoft: The dialogue highlights the tangible difference in witnessing Microsoft's innovation from the inside compared to the external perception. The speaker notes the depth and variety of projects, ranging from large-scale system developments to cutting-edge research and product innovations. This perspective introduces listeners to the energetic and intellectually stimulating environment within Microsoft.

Role of Core AI Team: The focus shifts to the specific contributions of the Core AI team, which the speaker leads. His explanation outlines the team’s goal of developing new technological frameworks and solutions driven by AI, central to Microsoft's adaptive and forward-thinking strategy. This effort resonates with Microsoft’s historical ethos of setting foundational technologies that empower builders and creators.

Developing and Adapting Technology: The discussion touches on the rapid evolution of AI technologies and the philosophical approach of remaining open to multiple possibilities and innovations. This adaptive strategy is essential for staying at the forefront of technological advancement, enabling Microsoft to cater to evolving user needs and opening new avenues for developers and customers alike.

Future Outlook: Finally, the conversation looks forward to the upcoming presentations and announcements planned for the continuation of the Build conference. Expectations are set for unveiling significant advancements that would encourage community engagement, feedback, and collaborative growth in utilizing AI and other technologies. The speaker emphasizes the cyclical nature of development, feedback, and enhancement in driving the tech community forward.

title: Building Secure Applications with Azure and Rust
sortTitle: building secure applications with azure and rust
description: Learn how the Azure SDK for Rust helps you build secure, memory-safe, and high-performance cloud applications. This session will showcase practical use cases, like managing secrets with Azure Key Vault, to demonstrate how you can create applications that are secure by default. Whether you're new to Rust or an experienced developer, this presentation offers valuable insights and examples to harness the power of Azure.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Secure Applications with Azure and Rust: The session was opened by welcoming participants to a discussion aimed at building secure applications using Azure and Rust. The speakers introduced themselves as seasoned professionals involved in Azure SDKs and leading engineering projects for Rust.

Adoption and Features of Rust in Azure: Rust was incorporated over a year ago into Azure's official SDK languages due to its safe memory management and performance capabilities. This inclusion helps prevent common bugs and enhances software reliability, which is crucial for security and efficiency in programming.

Release and Capabilities of the Azure SDK for Rust: The first beta versions of the Azure SDK for Rust were launched in February, including libraries for authentication, Azure Key Vault, Event Hubs, Cosmos DB, and storage blobs. These tools are designed to integrate well with Azure services, offering a streamlined developer experience and robust support for secure applications.

Security Features and Data Protection: The Azure SDK for Rust emphasizes security by design. It includes features that prevent the retention, caching, or reuse of security-sensitive data, protecting against information leaks. Additionally, the development team has employed custom traits to avoid accidental PII or sensitive information exposure in logs and debug outputs.

Focus on Developer Experience and Community Contribution: The Azure Rust SDK benefits from a growing ecosystem of libraries and tools, thanks to contributions from a vibrant community of developers both inside and outside Microsoft. These contributions ensure that the SDK remains robust, flexible, and up-to-date with the latest security standards and practices.

Engagement and Further Development: The speakers encouraged active participation from the audience to provide feedback and contribute to ongoing projects. The session concluded with an invitation to explore the repository and examples available, illustrating the practical uses of the Azure SDK for Rust in various applications.
location: Arch, 705 Pike, Level 4, Hub, Theater A

title: About Agents with Kendra Springer and Seth Juarez
sortTitle: about agents with kendra springer and seth juarez
description: Agents are everywhere, but are you getting the most out of them?  Kendra Springer and Seth Juarez discuss the key concepts and latest capabilities from Copilot Studio and Azure AI Foundry. Whether you're building, securing or deploying agents, you'll learn how to think about and utilize different types of agents in your business processes.
aiDescription:
Welcome Introduction: The transcript begins with a friendly greeting at a backstage event at Microsoft Build. The two speakers are excited to be together, expressing their enthusiasm about their partnership and the session they participated in the previous night.

Session Overview: They discuss a recent session that focused on large language models and their integration into agent-based systems. Despite the unconventional timing of the session, there was an emphasis on the importance and relevance of the content covered, particularly in relation to dinner plans which they humorously mention might have conflicted with the session timing.

Professional Roles and Responsibilities: The introduction of one of the speakers provides insight into her role as a Principal Group Product Manager. She elaborates on her responsibilities which include closely working with Microsoft account teams and customers, driving product positioning, and fostering the adoption and growth of Copilot Studio.

Discussion on Agent Development: The discussion transitions into a deeper exploration of building agents within systems. They address common questions regarding when and how to utilize Copilot Studio or build custom engine agents. This leads into a practical demonstration of agent creation, focusing on empowering users to integrate agents smoothly into their operational frameworks.

Demonstration and Explanation: The practical part of the transcript involves a detailed demonstration on creating an agent, specifically a procurement advisor agent. Using Copilot Studio, they showcase how to initiate and configure an agent using a structured prompt and integrating knowledge from SharePoint sites. This highlights the ease and intuitive nature of building agents tailored to specific business needs.

Interactive Feedback and Conclusions: Towards the end, there is an interactive segment where the agent is tested in real-time scenarios, illustrating its capability to adhere to procurement guidelines efficiently. This portion underscores the agent's ability to automate and streamline complex processes, potentially saving significant time and resources in a business setting. The session wraps up with a reflection on the power and potential of such technology in enhancing productivity and operational efficiency.

title: Transform Cancer Care Management with Multimodal AI Agents
sortTitle: transform cancer care management with multimodal ai agents
description: Use Azure AI Foundry and Copilot Studio to build and deploy multimodal AI agents that integrate Health and Life Sciences models with Microsoft 365. Customize agents to expedite complex cancer care management tasks like structuring medical data, generating patient timelines, conducting literature reviews, matching clinical trials, generating reports, and more. Learn how to modify and integrate these agents while connecting to Azure AI Foundry, Microsoft Fabric, and public sources.

title: Enhancing Microsoft Intune Security with Modern Authentication
sortTitle: enhancing microsoft intune security with modern authentication
description: Join us for a hands-on demo exploring modern authentication methods that eliminate the need for storing passwords. Learn how to leverage client credentials to secure your applications without relying on delegated scopes. We'll walk through best practices, implementation strategies, and real-world examples to help you enhance your security posture and streamline authentication processes.
aiDescription:
Welcome and Introduction: The session opened with a welcome to the webinar titled "Enhancing Microsoft Intune Security with Modern Authentication." The focus was on modern authentication methods that eliminate the need for storing passwords, leveraging client credentials, and avoiding reliance on delegated scopes. The presenters prepared participants for a hands-on demonstration and discussed implementation strategies and best practices.

Team Introduction: Presenters introduced themselves as members of the Microsoft Security Customer Experience Engineering team. They elaborated on their role in developing and enhancing customer experiences across various Microsoft security products, including Intune, MDE, Purview, and Sentinel.

Demo Scenario Setup: A hypothetical scenario was set at Alpine Ski House, a winter sports company aiming to bolster its digital security. The focus was on implementing modern authentication methods like multi-factor authentication (MFA) and biometric verification to ensure secure access to their systems.

Authentication Technologies Explained: The presenters outlined the use of client credentials and managed identities in securing applications. These methods minimize the risks associated with credential management and ensure secure interactions within digital environments.

Hands-On Demonstration: The hands-on demo involved using a certificate to authenticate securely to Microsoft Graph, avoiding password storage and unnecessary user privileges. The process highlighted the role of modern authentication technologies in enhancing security posture and streamlining authentication processes.

Conclusion and Q&A: The session concluded with a summary of the insights shared on strengthening security within Microsoft Intune environments using modern authentication. Participants were encouraged to engage further through comments and feedback for additional queries and discussions.

title: Optimize your Azure environment with performance insights from Datadog
sortTitle: optimize your azure environment with performance insights from datadog
description: Datadog is a unified observability and security platform that provides organizations with full-stack observability into metrics, logs and traces. Learn how the platform can give you insights into the performance and health of Azure technologies like Azure OpenAI, Azure Functions, Azure DevOps, Azure SQL Database and more so that you can keep your Azure environment in tip top shape.

title: Run AI Agent pipelines privately on Ryzen AI PCs
sortTitle: run ai agent pipelines privately on ryzen ai pcs
description: This workshop introduces GAIA, an open-source application for running efficient agentic RAG pipelines on Ryzen AI hardware. Learn to leverage Lemonade LLM server for hybrid iGPU/NPU execution, generate synthetic evaluation data, implement LLM-as-judge methodologies for pipeline tuning, and deploy agentic reasoning to enhance retrieval quality within consumer PC hardware constraints.

title: Unlock cloud migration success
sortTitle: unlock cloud migration success
description: Migrating on-prem applications to Azure offers better scalability, security, and cost efficiency, while enabling teams to explore new technologies like GenAI. But success takes more than moving workloads—it requires visibility into current systems, monitoring during traffic shifts, and ensuring a great user experience post-migration. This session covers practical strategies for tracking cloud migrations, including challenges from LLM-enabled apps.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Cloud Migration: The session began with an introduction from the speaker detailing the focus on strategies for successful cloud migration. The conversation aimed to unpack the complexities and benefits associated with moving to cloud-based infrastructure, addressing the concerns most organizations face during such transitions.

The Rise of Cloud Adoption: The speaker presented data from a Gartner report indicating a significant surge in cloud adoption, with a 20% increase in public cloud spending in the previous year alone. This segment underscored the widespread industry shift towards cloud services, setting the stage for a deeper discussion on why businesses are making this move and the potential hurdles they may encounter.

Challenges in Cloud Migration: Three primary challenges were outlined: the large scale of applications, heavy customization, and insufficient skills within teams for cloud migration. These factors contribute complexity and can impede seamless transition to cloud platforms. The discourse emphasized the need for organizations to upskill their workforce and plan meticulously to navigate these obstacles effectively.

Benefits of Moving to the Cloud: The discussion highlighted several advantages of cloud migration, including scalability, reliability, security, cost optimization, and resource management. Each of these benefits was explored in detail, explaining how cloud environments support business growth, enhance operational efficiency, and improve service delivery through advanced security measures and scalable resources.

Migrating with Strategy and Best Practices: Various strategies like re-hosting, re-platforming, repurchasing, and re-architecting were discussed, each applicable depending on the specific needs and conditions of a business. Additionally, the speaker introduced tools and frameworks, such as Azure Migrate and the Cloud Adoption Framework, which support organizations in planning and executing migration effectively.

Conclusion and Resources: The session concluded with an emphasis on the importance of thorough preparation and understanding of one's own infrastructure and applications before initiating cloud migration. The speaker pointed attendees towards additional resources for further learning and guided next steps, ensuring they are well-equipped for their cloud migration journey.

title: Build smarter reports directly in Visual Studio with ActiveReports.NET
sortTitle: build smarter reports directly in visual studio with activereports.net
description: Discover how to build rich, powerful reports entirely within Visual Studio using ActiveReports.NET. In this session, Product Manager Alec Gall demonstrates how the integrated designer streamlines report development—no context switching required. Watch a live walkthrough of creating a dynamic report and see how easy it is to design, preview, and deploy reports without leaving your .NET development environment.
aiDescription:
Overview of ActiveReports.NET: The presentation is delivered by a product manager who introduces ActiveReports.NET, a .NET reporting tool designed specifically for developers. This tool allows for the creation of smarter reports directly within Visual Studio, intending to streamline the reporting experience.

Challenges Addressed: Common problems faced by developers, such as context switching, the feeling of reports being an afterthought, the disconnected nature of report design from codebase, and inconsistency in reporting when working in teams are discussed. These challenges can lead to increased development friction and inconsistencies that ActiveReports.NET aims to resolve.

Seamless Integration Features: ActiveReports.NET offers a fully integrated reporting solution in Visual Studio, providing features like drag-and-drop report designers. This integration allows developers to remain within the IDE (Integrated Development Environment) throughout the development process, thus minimizing errors and enhancing productivity through features such as debugging and access to application code.

Demonstration of Report Creation: A detailed step-by-step walkthrough is provided on creating a functional and dynamic report using ActiveReports.NET's Visual Studio integrated designer. The demonstration covers data linking, layout building, styling, and final adjustments to produce a professionally styled report without leaving the IDE.

Advanced Reporting Tools: The speaker showcases advanced functionalities such as conditional formatting and dynamic data visualizations within a report. These features are demonstrated to highlight how users can design reports that are not only informative but also interactive, enhancing the decision-making process based on the report data presented.

Conclusion and Additional Resources: The session concludes with an invitation to download a free trial of ActiveReports.NET and explore further resources such as tutorials, demos, and community forums for additional support. The presenter encourages viewers to engage with their support team for personalized demos or to integrate ActiveReports.NET into their specific architecture, enriching the overall development experience within Visual Studio.

title: Lightning-fast analytical queries on massive datasets with ClickHouse
sortTitle: lightning-fast analytical queries on massive datasets with clickhouse
description: Extracting real-time insights from massive datasets is a growing challenge. ClickHouse combines columnar storage and best-in-class compression to produce unparalleled query speed at any scale. Learn how companies like Microsoft, Sony, eBay, and Lyft leverage ClickHouse for applications ranging from user-facing dashboards to ad-hoc analyses over petabytes of data.
aiDescription:
Introduction and Overview: The discussion introduces ClickHouse, a column-oriented, distributed Online Analytical Processing (OLAP) database that excels in aggregating large datasets efficiently. ClickHouse supports fluent SQL aligned with the ANSI standard and offers impressive data ingestion and querying capabilities, alongside robust architecture that includes features like replication, sharding, and cross-region support.

Historical Milestones: After its inception in 2009, ClickHouse was open-sourced in 2016. The establishment of ClickHouse as a VC-backed company occurred in 2021, followed by significant funding exceeding $300 million. The platform has rapidly gained popularity, evidenced by its strong performance in community growth and industry rankings.

Practical Implementations: ClickHouse caters to a variety of data-intensive applications across multiple domains including real-time analytics for customer applications, data lakes, warehousing, SQL-based observability, and increasingly machine learning and AI. Notably, the platform's capabilities support high concurrency and robust integrations with analytical tools and datasets.

Performance and Cost-Efficiency: A key advantage of ClickHouse is its performance efficiency, enabling millions of data row ingestions per second without compromising on query speed—a crucial feature for real-time data analysis applications. ClickHouse’s cost model is also highlighted as favorable, avoiding per-query charges which can be exorbitant, particularly with heavy data loads.

Cloud Capabilities and Extensions: The ClickHouse Cloud offers a managed, serverless option that simplifies deployment across major cloud service providers. The database management system efficiently handles heavy workloads, ensuring scalability without the need to provision excessive server resources. Additionally, ClickHouse continues to adapt and extend its features, including better integration with external data sources and formats like Delta Lake and Iceberg.

Summary of Benefits: Conclusively, ClickHouse is portrayed as a highly capable analytics database that stands out for its speed, scalability, and adaptability to modern data needs, from simple applications to complex, distributed cloud environments. It is designed with the considerations of the future of data analytics, supporting both the operational demands and strategic data decision-making for businesses.

title: Tips for fast vector and full-text search with Azure Cosmos DB
sortTitle: tips for fast vector and full-text search with azure cosmos db
description: Enable smarter and more intuitive information discovery in AI apps and analytics with Azure Cosmos DB. This coding session will show ways to balance accuracy and speed with full-text search for precise keyword matching; vector search for semantic understanding that captures the meaning behind queries, and hybrid search which uses the strengths of both to deliver enhanced relevance and contextual accuracy.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Presenters and Azure Cosmos DB: The webinar featured a discussion with two experts from the Azure Cosmos DB team. They aimed to share efficient strategies for implementing vector, full text, and hybrid search functionalities in Cosmos DB, which are recently enhanced features of the database.

Capabilities and Performance: Cosmos DB's scalability and the ability to handle complex search scenarios at scale were highlighted. The database can manage over a billion vectors with less than 100 milliseconds latency. Thanks to built-in vector indexing and powerful underlying architecture, Cosmos DB offers cost-effectiveness up to 41 times less than comparable solutions.

Enhancements and Global Features: The presenters discussed recent global updates including enterprise-grade robustness with features like 5-nines of availability, geo-replication, and fleet management in preview for DB containers. These features significantly enhance the reliability and effectiveness of database operations across different scenarios.

Efficiency, Throughput, and Scalability: Discussing the technical setup, the speakers emphasized the importance of provisioning sufficient throughput and utilizing features like bulk execution for efficient data ingestion. Azure Cosmos DB's auto-scaling capabilities and partition querying contribute to its superior performance and ability to manage large-scale operations efficiently.

Hybrid Search and Flexibility: They further showcased the hybrid search capability that combines vector and full-text search, providing users flexibility to tune queries for desired accuracy or latency. The ability to dynamically adjust parameters according to specific needs was also discussed, illustrating Cosmos DB's adaptability.

Demo and Practical Insights: A live demonstration highlighted the implementation of the mentioned features, showing actual code and the effect of different settings on query performance. This hands-on approach helped in understanding the practical application of the theoretical concepts discussed throughout the webinar.

title: Simplify AI development maximizing the power of local models and MCPs
sortTitle: simplify ai development maximizing the power of local models and mcps
description:
Local LLM development is no longer a niche—it’s becoming a necessity. Developers are increasingly choosing to run models locally to gain better performance, reduce costs, and maintain data privacy. But despite the growing demand, the local AI development experience remains fragmented and complex. In this session, you’ll get a first look at how Docker is streamlining local AI workflow. 
We’ll also explore Docker’s support for the Model Context Protocol (MCP)—a new standard that defines how appli
aiDescription:
Docker Model Runner Introduction: Michael Irwin from the DevRel team at Docker discusses enhancing AI development through new tools in Docker's lineup, emphasizing Docker Model Runner. This tool is designed to streamline AI model deployment, allowing local execution with full GPU access to speed up processes, and is integrated with a new OCI artifact specification to manage model distribution independently from inference engines.

Model Deployment and Management: Docker has facilitated model deployment through Docker Hub, where a suite of models from various sources can be accessed and managed. Developers can use a CLI to pull, list, and run models from Docker Hub, with the process being demonstrated using a local cache to expedite loading and execution, showcasing the efficiency of local model operation.

Handling GPU Utilization: Docker Model Runner addresses the challenge of GPU-enabled container execution for AI models by enabling direct execution on the host machine instead of inside containers. This approach not only circumvents the complexities associated with container-based GPU access but also ensures better performance and resource utilization.

Tooling and API Integration: Docker extends familiar command-line tools to interact with AI models, ensuring a seamless integration into existing development workflows. The Model Runner exposes models through OpenAI API-compatible endpoints, allowing them to be accessed via standard HTTP requests and effortlessly integrated with external software and services.

Enhanced Control and Security: The introduction of Docker Business' Registry Access Management tool within the Docker environment adds a layer of security. It ensures that models accessed and deployed within a business's infrastructure are secure and authorized, aligning with the increasing need for secure model management solutions in AI development spaces.

Future Tool Integrations and Updates: Docker is actively developing additional tools and functionalities around AI model deployment and management to further simplify and enhance the developers' experience. These future updates are anticipated to introduce more refined commands and integrations, aiming to keep up with the evolving needs of AI development and deployment.

title: Python Meets .NET: Building AI Solutions with Combined Strengths
sortTitle: python meets .net: building ai solutions with combined strengths
description: .NET is the ideal platform for building fast, scalable, Enterprise-ready apps that run anywhere. Python is the ideal platform for doing data-science, analytics, and machine-learning. .NET runs some of the biggest applications on the planet and Python has been used for some of the biggest scientific discoveries of the past 20 years. In this session, Scott Hanselman and Anthony Shaw will discuss and demo how to spice-up your .NET applications with Python.
aiDescription:
Introduction to Sea Snakes: Sea Snakes is an open-source project designed to integrate Python capabilities seamlessly into .NET applications, allowing developers to utilize Python libraries directly within their .NET code. The technology facilitates the interoperability between .NET and Python, making it possible to run Python code within .NET processes without the need for separate Python installations or configurations.

Performance and Compatibility: A key focus for Sea Snakes has been to maintain high performance and compatibility with ongoing .NET and Python updates. The tool aims to handle heavy-duty tasks like AI and ML model integrations smoothly, leveraging .NET’s advanced features such as memory spans and type-safe bindings to optimize system resources and performance.

Use Case Demonstrations: Demonstrations highlight how Sea Snakes can be applied practically, including interfacing with AI models from platforms like Hugging Face. These demos illustrate the ease of fetching and running external models, like Facebook's DETR and audio processing with Whisper, directly in .NET applications, essentially treating Python’s broad library ecosystem as an extension of .NET capabilities.

Development and Community: Sea Snakes is developed collaboratively by multiple contributors, emphasizing an open development model. Contributions and feedback are actively encouraged via GitHub, where developers can engage, report issues, and request features. This community-driven approach ensures the tool evolves in line with the actual needs and workflows of modern developers.

Future Directions and Events: Looking towards the future, Sea Snakes is positioned to continue evolving, supporting the latest versions of .NET and Python, and expanding its capabilities in line with developer feedback. Upcoming events like .NET Conf 2025 and developer conferences provide opportunities for the community to learn more about Sea Snakes and other innovative .NET tools and frameworks.

title: Better Microservices Development using Azure Container Apps
sortTitle: better microservices development using azure container apps
description: Life is hard. Building microservices doesn't have to be. In this code-focused talk, we will show you how to build and maintain distributed Container Apps with less time, effort, and pain. We will show you what’s new, go beyond getting started and dive deep into production scenarios. We will identify and fix performance issues live on stage using observability tools like the Aspire dashboard, then show you how to improve the functionality and resiliency of your app with open-source tech like KEDA. aiDescription:
Introduction to Build Conference: The session is described as one of the most valuable and exciting at the Build conference, promising fast coding and live demos.
Azure Container Apps (ACA): ACA is highlighted as a developer-first service that empowers developers to deliver fast, from zero innovation to hyperscale.
Session Agenda: The session includes three live demos by different presenters, showcasing various aspects of ACA.
Getting Started with ACA: Sneha Parthasarathy, an engineer on the ACA team, demonstrates how easy it is to get started with ACA, including rule-based routing, logging, and telemetry.
Cloud Petting Zoo Demo: The demo app consists of five microservices deployed as container apps in the ACA environment, with a front-end React application using rule-based routing.
Deployment and Scaling: The deployment process involves using the Azure CLI to create container apps, setting up scaling configurations, and monitoring health probes.
Telemetry and Debugging: The session covers how ACA uses OpenTelemetry (otel) for logging, tracing, and metrics, and demonstrates debugging a live site incident.
NFL Combine App: Izzy from Southworks showcases how ACA was used to build AI capabilities for the NFL Combine app, enhancing the experience with AI-driven insights.
Solution Architecture: The architecture of the NFL Combine app includes a React JS front-end, an API-driven backend hosted in ACA, integration with Azure AI, and data storage in Cosmos DB.
Enterprise Capabilities: The session announces new enterprise features for ACA, including premium ingress, rule-based routing, private endpoints, and durable task scheduler.

Interactive Environment: The session also covered the benefits of the Azure AI Foundry which now includes a dedicated video playground. This new feature supports interactive experimentation, allowing users to refine video concepts dynamically, showcasing the immediate value of integrating video generation in various business workflows.

Accessibility and Integration: The session placed a strong emphasis on the ease of integrating Sora’s capabilities into various applications. This is achievable through the Sora API, which provides a seamless bridge from the Azure Open AI service to user-specific applications, enhancing creative workflows and operational efficiencies.

Q&A and Conclusion: The session concluded with a vibrant Q&A, where speakers fielded questions from the audience, providing deeper insights into Sora's operational mechanisms and potential applications. The session reassured attendees of continuous support and updates, encouraging them to begin utilizing these advanced tools within their own platforms.

speakerNames: Marco Casalaina, Jennifer Marsman, Cosmin Voicu
title: Solving the Unsolvable: Advanced Agentic AI Models in Azure AI Foundry
sortTitle: solving the unsolvable: advanced agentic ai models in azure ai foundry
description: What if AI could go beyond prediction—and truly reason and act? Explore next-gen models like OpenAI’s o1 and o3 mini, computer-use agent (CUA), DeepSeek R1 and V3, all in Azure AI Foundry. Using techniques like chain-of-thought prompting and “long thinking,” these models tackle complex, multi-step problems with depth and accuracy, and take actions. See how agentic models are transforming industries like healthcare, finance, and customer service—unlocking a new era of intelligent applications.
aiDescription:
**Introduction to AI Models in Azure AI Foundry:**
During the session, advanced agentic AI models in Azure AI Foundry were discussed. The speaker outlined the agenda, focusing on new developments in reasoning models and computer use agents.

**Overview of Reasoning Models:**
Reasoning models have gained attention for their ability to handle more complex problem-solving compared to conventional models. These models employ sophisticated reasoning mechanisms, making them adept at tasks that require deep analytical processing and generating programmed outputs.

**Capabilities of Reasoning Models:**
Participants learned about the enhanced capabilities of reasoning models such as generating programs, comparing documents in detail, and identifying nuanced data patterns. These models provide insights into vast amounts of text, highlighting their capacity to manage intricacies that traditional models might overlook.

**Application and Development Tools:**
There was an emphasis on practical application, guiding the audience on how to implement these models for complex problem-solving. Suggestions were made to explore the Azure AI Foundry for a variety of reasoning models, implying the community and resources available for continued learning and application.

**Limitations and Considerations:**
Despite their advanced capabilities, the models come with higher operational complexities and costs. It was noted that while they are powerful, developers should consider the scenario requirements before implementation to ensure cost-effectiveness and efficiency.

**Conclusion and Encouragement:**
The session concluded with encouragement for participants to leverage these advanced AI models to enhance their applications. Attendees were urged to further explore and experiment with these tools within their respective projects to fully harness their potential in the real-world scenarios.

speakerNames: Ehrik Aldana, Meeri Haataja, Mehrnoosh Sameki, Veera Siivonen
title: From risk to reward: AI governance integrations for Azure AI Foundry
sortTitle: from risk to reward: ai governance integrations for azure ai foundry
description: Azure AI Foundry is proud to partner with Credo AI and Saidot to help customers tackle the end to end AI governance. Join us for a demo of how Azure AI Foundry integrates with these companies' AI governance platforms to help organizations build a centralized view of their AI models and use cases, identify relevant risks, run evaluations, and apply relevant mitigations to deliver more trustworthy AI apps.
aiDescription:
Introduction and Context: The transcript begins with a warm welcome from the host who introduces themselves and their role at Azure AI Foundry. They specify their involvement with AI system evaluations and governance, highlighting the launch of a new, significant integration.

Guests and Purpose: The session includes key partners from leading AI governance companies, expressing a strong alliance and mutual excitement for the showcased integrations. These partnerships are framed as essential for enhancing AI applications' evaluation regarding quality, safety, and performance.

Integration Goals: The primary aim of this integration, as outlined, is to bridge the existing gaps in AI system evaluations by introducing a governance layer. This facilitates effective communication and collaboration between AI developers and governance bodies, ensuring compliance and enhancing reliability.

Demonstration Start: One of the collaborators begins a demonstration, intending to showcase the practical implementation of the governance tools in real-world scenarios. They specifically focus on an AI-driven chatbot for HR purposes, emphasizing the system's registration, risk management, and evaluation setups within an AI governance framework.

Evaluation Process: The transcript elaborates on generating evaluation plans specific to the system's context, highlighting how these plans align with the identified risks and the compliance requirements. There's a detailed explanation about the use of targeted evaluations to assess risks and implement necessary adjustments aimed at optimizing the system's deployment in HR environments.

Closing and Transition: Following a thorough showcase of the evaluation process, the transcript transitions to another segment where another partner company is set to demonstrate additional integrations, reaffirming the ongoing collaborations aimed at refining AI applications through structured governance and evaluation mechanisms.
location: Arch, 705 Pike, Level 4, Hub, Theater A

title: Building Resilient Cloud-Native Microservices
sortTitle: building resilient cloud-native microservices
description: Learn how to build resilient cloud-native microservices using Dapr and modern Azure hosting solutions like AKS and ACA. This session will demonstrate strategies for managing distributed transactions using the Saga pattern and ensuring data consistency through eventual consistency. It will focus on practical demos and actionable insights for building robust cloud-native systems.
aiDescription:
Introduction and Customer Scenario: The transcript introduces a situation where a customer, John, wants to rent a car, illustrating the simplicity of the process from a user's perspective. However, the system's backend is complex, involving multiple microservices, each with its state, creating challenges in maintaining system consistency when one microservice fails.

System Complexity and Microservices: As the narrative progresses, the speaker, an experienced software developer and owner of a software house, explains the transition from a monolithic architecture to microservices. This shift aims to enhance scalability and availability but complicates ensuring system consistency across various states and services.

The Saga Pattern: To address these complexities, the concept of the Saga pattern is introduced. This pattern helps manage distributed transactions across multiple microservices by orchestrating the flow and handling failures through compensatory actions, thus aiming for eventual consistency.

Implementation and Tools: The speaker discusses implementing the Saga pattern using Dapper, a platform that supports cloud-native applications and facilitates microservices architecture. Dapper aids in maintaining service state, which is crucial for asynchronous processes like Sagas. Examples of using Dapper and other tools, such as Docker and Azure, for deploying applications are presented to provide practical insights into building and managing complex systems.

Challenges and Solutions: Through a demonstration, the speaker shows how the system handles various scenarios, including failures. The ability of the Saga pattern to revert actions if operations like billing fail is highlighted. This feature ensures that the system can maintain reliability and consistency, even in error scenarios.

Conclusion and Future Directions: Finally, the transcript concludes with thoughts on the potential future integration of AI in orchestration processes to enhance automation and decision-making in complex systems. The speaker invites feedback on the discussed methodologies and suggests further exploration of the materials through additional resources like a downloadable book on the architecture and Saga pattern.
location: Arch, 705 Pike, Level 4, Hub, Theater A

sortTitle: accelerate ai app development with ai gateway capabilities in azure api management
description:

In this hands-on lab, you’ll explore how to leverage AI gateway capabilities to streamline traffic management, control token usage, and leverage semantic caching to improve API performance and reduce costs. You’ll dive into key features such as load balancing, token management, and semantic caching, learning how to simplify AI API consumption, enhance app performance, and optimize costs. By the end of the session, you’ll be equipped with the skills to integrate large language models (LLMs) seamlessly, manage high-volume API traffic, and build more efficient AI-powered apps.

title: Deep Research with Langchain and DeepSeek R1
sortTitle: deep research with langchain and deepseek r1
description:
In this workshop we will build a Deep Researcher. Give it a topic and it will generate a web search query, gather web search results, summarize the results of web search, reflect on the summary to examine knowledge gaps, generate a new search query to address the gaps, search, and improve the summary for a user-defined number of cycles. It will provide the user a final markdown summary with all sources used. We will use DeepSeek R1 as the LLM, and Langchain to implement reflection architecture.
